true to size The material is soft and stretchy, thicker than typical jeggings. They keep their shape thru several wears, no baginess at the knees. Cold wash, low heat dry with no issues. 5'4", size 6/8, wearing the 8. I would not consider these "wide leg", more like a straight boyfriend fit. I like the off-center buttons and zipper but wish it was more exaggerated. The strip of fabric for the topmost button is a bit thin. When there's a lot of movement or while pulling up the waist, my tummy peeks through. I'm now a true size 6 and love how they look. The fit is more baggy and slouchy and has a relaxed off-beat vibe. I would suggest ordering 1 size up.

Great Style and Comfort, Inseam a Bit Short for Taller Ladies These casual pants from KICZOY are so unique and made very nicely! The denim is soft and has a nice stretch to the fabric. If you are looking for a body-hugging fit along with true comfort, you will want to check these out. I also love the design. I think the front with its asymmetrical look is so flattering, and the back has seams that give the backside shape. The only issue I have with these pants is the length. They run a bit short for my taste. For size reference, I'm 5'6" and 127 lbs. I ordered a size 6 and feel they are true to size. I just wish they were an inch or so longer. Still, they are a nicely constructed garment with a fresh style that I welcome into my fall wardrobe. I have included photos to try to give you a closer look and sincerely hope that they help you out a bit with your decision. Thank you!

Quality belt! All pros!! Very nice, well made belt. It came in a nice box, which was great for gifting. It is real leather, and has a cutting device if you need to shorten it. It was packaged very well with all the bells, and whistles of a designer belt. I wasn't sure if my daughter would need the small or medium based on the size guide, so I went with the medium since it did come with the option to shorten if needed, and the medium was the right choice. For reference, my daughter has an athletic build. She is almost 5'4, and 132 lbs. She wears a size 7 in juniors. I hope this gives you a good idea for sizing. It's a very nice black, real leather, belt. It's not too thin, or too wide. I imagine it will fit any belt loop size, but still has a nice wide width. I will post pics of the belt when I get home. I would definitely, reccomend this, and will probably purchase in other colors when available.

Comfortable Sweater The color combination (burgungy and white) of this sweater is very nice and goes well with jeans as well as leggings. Beautiful and True to size Ok, so first things first, I noticed alot of people in the reviews complained about the size. These are a rigid denim, which means NO stretch, they are true to size,so if you're normally a 30, but wear a 28 in stretchy jeans...order the 30. I'm 4'11" and around 115 and the 24 fits like a glove!! There are no gaps at the waist, I love the cute little detail at the bottom of the leg, and your butt will never look better than in a pair of 501's!! These jeans are beautiful with adorable distressing and the 5 button fly I've come to love in a pair of Levi's jeans. I had these in my cart ans caught them for $20 on sale and wish I had ordered a few more. These are a great fit with a straight leg, Snug through the hips and thighs. You're going to love these jeans!!

ACTUAL low rise jeans!! Ok, first of all, these are authentic. All pockets are real pockets, and they are extremely well made. I'm 5'0", 108 lbs, and I bought the 27s. The fit is PERFECT, but I will definitely need to have them hemmed. For now I've been folding the excess pant leg up and under, and it works just fine, because the fit is slim enough that it stays in place all day without budging.I can't gush enough about how thrilled I am to have found these jeans. The high rise pant trend needs to die. It's not really flattering on anyone, but it is murder on us short girls. At only 5'0", even "low rise" jeans come up so high on me that the waist band always covers my belly button. Some "low rise" jeans come up so high on my torso I can practically tuck my tits into them. Not only is it crazy unflattering, but it's incredibly uncomfortable. The jeans always dug into my stomach when I sat down. For years I have had to rearrange the waistband of my jeans every time I sat. I so missed the 90's style, "sit so low on your hips you flash your crack every time you sit" jeans. They were so much more comfortable and flattering on me! I've been searching for years, but couldn't find anyone that still makes a true "sits on the hips" jean. And then I found these! I couldn't believe it when I put them on. After trying on hundreds of pairs of "low rise" jeans that came up to above my belly button, these actually sit right at my hip bone, right where they should. And unlike the 90's low rise jeans, these fit so well they don't have that weird gap in the back of the waistband when you sit, and they don't flash your undies. They stay put. They are AMAZING. So flattering and comfortable.The only slight drawback is that the back pockets do sit a pretty low, so they're not the most flattering butt jeans ever. And if you're someone who is constantly shoving your phone in your back pocket like I am, the pocket flap is not the most convenient thing ever. But those are minor issues for me, considering how overall thrilled I am with these. After the first pair arrived and I saw how well they fit I immediately ordered two more pairs, and they are equally amazing. Love 'em.

Very cute ❤️ I’m obsessed w jeans that have back flap pockets. Mainly bc I have no booty and back flap pockets gives the illusion of a little more in the trunk. They are a darker blue Jean w a red stitch just like picture and has a cute tiny pocket in front (see pic). These jeans are low rise but manageable enough due to the decent stretch material not to give u much muffin top. They are stretchy but not jeggings and do not sag after wearing all day which is a huge thing for me. ! Material is comfortable and soft. It’s not a heavy Jean but not a thin one either. I have two pairs of these Stella jeans (another in black) both low rise. I was nervous about size but I went with my usual 26 and they fit perfectly. In juniors I’m usually 7 or 5 they are just long enough and I’m 5’3” 136 lbs. These jeans were worn all day before I took these pictures. They do wrinkle up a bit in the butt (maybe cuz I don’t have one) and looks like I have underarm on. I do not. Still a cute jean .. wash in cold hang to dry. See pics.

NOT dusty orange; otherwise good This is a tunic-length (depending on your height/proportions, of course) t-shirt, essentially. I thought it would have more drape like a very lightweight sweater, but it doesn't. It's ok, I actually like it a lot. I'm not one to cover my rear (I still like my booty), but I recently bought a couple pairs of unreturnable leggings that give major vpl, so I thought I'd get this for walking the dog around the block for our "stretch your leg" walks (not working out/running). I'm 5'4" and have a long torso and short legs, and this is exact tunic length on me, with the pockets hitting just slightly lower than would be *perfect* for me, personally. They'll be perfect for a lot of other people. I ordered by their size chart and it fits as expected. The sleeves are a lot less "poofy" than some of the pictures make it look. It's a looong drop-sleeve, so the seam is even farther down the arm (near the elbow) than a traditional drop-sleeve (which falls nearer the bicep), and I don't hate it right now. It's not the softest seam, as some others have pointed out, nor is there much (or any?) stretch to it, so it's a bit awkward to get it situated on the arm, but it's not enough to make me not wear it. Catch me when I have a bad eczema outbreak and that might be a different story. I am a bit annoyed with the color, however. It called this "burnt orange" when, in fact, it's very, very red-orange...very bright. There were no reviews when I purchased it, but now there's another reviewer who said the same thing. They didn't have as favorable a view on it (I happen to love bright colors, poppy orange/red being at the top of the list), and likened it to a traffic cone, so I was hoping there was more red in it to make it just bright, not construction-site-orange. It arrived and, thankfully, it's a very red-orange and I asked 3 other people if they thought of a traffic cone and they all said 'no.' So, at least for my color vision, it's an acceptable shade of orange for me. I absolutely took points off for it being grossly misrepresented, though. While I'll wear the color, it's not what I ordered nor is it what they showed. There's no way monitors got the color this off - it's a completely different tone/color/shade, this isn't a monitor-viewing thing. I'll have way less versatility with this color, which means it'll probably get donated rather than worn out, and I hate contributing to the huge clothing-waste issue. $20 at time of purchase (8/21/24) through Vine, so I can't return/exchange, otherwise I would think about getting another color they couldn't mess up.

Not exactly “batwing”, but cute. Here’s why I give this tunic dress four stars: * as alluded to in the title of this review, the top part for the bat wing is not very exaggerated. In fact, I wouldn’t be able to tell that it was that style if I didn’t know when I ordered it. * that said, it is a very cute dress * I like the fabric a lot. In weight, it is between a T-shirt and a thin sweater. It is ribbed and very stretchy. * I love the two pockets on the front! * I ordered a size medium. I would’ve been better in a size small. For reference, I am 5 foot two, approximately 110 pounds. BOTTOM LINE: This is a very cute and versatile dress, and I highly recommend it.

Vine reviewer here giving you my honest hands-on review and inspection of the product you are considering. I ordered a small, I wear a 10 and it has some room for me to move, but too tight for me and the look I want. I am losing weight, so I plan on fitting into this by winter. So, if you are an 8, a small or medium will probably fit nicely, depending how loose you want it. At a size 10, I should have ordered a medium or large. I feel the sizing chart is accurate. The color is dark, dark blue, as is on the image online. My pics lighten the color a lot. It is long and comes past my fingertips. I am short, 5'2", so a tall person will still get that tunic look. It is made with a thin, tightly woven, quality knit fabric: very stretchy and super comfy. Even with the tunic being a little too tight, the fabric was forgiving and not clingy. The crew neck is just the right size. It doesn't come up too high, making your neck look short. The sleeves are just a little different. They are in two pieces. This works. They are snug, yet still very comfortale. You can bunch them and the stay. I like them a lot. Of course, the pockets in the front are a big win, and they lay flat and don't add bulk. Overall, a nice knit tunic.

Good Basic! I like this shirtdress overall. It is fairly short and so I will be wearing leggings underneath it (I have a longer torso, 5'8", 160 lbs). I got my usual size and would say it's true to size or, if anything, a big oversized. It's very roomy. That makes it comfy or possible to wear a belt for a more fitted look. I like the fabric - thick but not too "crunchy" and soft enough to be comfy. Fabric is a mix of cotton and tencel. A good basic staple for your wardrobe!

Love it!! First, this is REAL jean material!!! I love this! I’m 5’6 ordered a large because I’m also 3 months post partum and wanted a looser fit. It fits true to size, is not short at all which I was worried about, don’t want my booty hanging out, and it’s SO comfortable! This is totally worth the price. You don’t find many quality denim pieces anymore and this truly is one. You could totally wear this a handful of different ways and this will honestly be a closet staple for me for years to come!

Just the right amount of baggy comfort I ordered these in the medium (unisex) size––I’m 5’5”, 140lbs and I found the medium to be the perfect amount of baggy without swimming in them so much that I feel like I have no shape. The elastic on both the ankle and waist is nice and not at all tight; the drawstring is just a simple cotton shoe-lace style rope; and there are no grommets to keep the string from disappearing into the elastic waistband. For me, the backside is a little saggy, but that’ll be different for everyone. Plus it will shrink over time because it’s cotton. The fabric is medium to lightweight, it’s slightly fleeced on the inside and a mostly soft outside with some texture to it, which is similar to a fine, corduroy stripe. Pepper is a great, dusty dark grey that goes with everything. I’m happy enough with them that I’m thinking of also buying them in blue.

Comfortable, No flaws, Nice sized pockets Shipping was quick for these blue-ish sweats. They also came with a tag "Comfort Colors" that warns they might fade in the wash. They did fade a little but it wasn't much - still good to know so you don't ruin something light/white. I did put a white washcloth in to test the colorfastness. They also got softer after being washed, making them my new favorite sweats! The color of these isn't a true blue, but more of a dusty, greyish, blue. Very nice color though. The weave on the fabric is interesting. Another review akinned it to corduroy. You can see a slight wale in it but it isn't stiff or really noticeable unless you look closely. The inseam was about 29" which fits me perfectly at 190# and 5'8". The drawstring in these is a solid material similar to a shoelace - no stretch - but long enough to sinch in if you need it. Good sized pockets which I recommend turning inside out for the first was at least as my pockets had quite a fluff collection going on in there lol Overall I love them. Super comfortable. Just the right length.

I sized up because these are 100% cotton. Very comfortable, relaxed fit I recently got a pair of these grey (“pepper”) 100% cotton sweatpants. I decided to size up to an XL in case they shrink. Ordinarily, I wear a M or L depending on the brand, and for reference, I am 6’ tall and weigh 150 lbs. These sweatpants are medium weight, seem well made, and have a relaxed fit that is very comfortable. The waist is slightly large on me, but the rest of the fit is perfect, and there is a drawstring to keep them in place. I usually wear a 32-34” waist, and these are a bit bigger than that, which makes sense because I sized up to an XL. The pockets are medium to small in size, the length is good, and I expect these will get a lot of use.

GREAT "SKIN" TIGHT JEANS WITH LOTS OF STRETCH.. These American Apparel Easy Jeans are exactly what my daughter wanted. She is extremely tall and skinny for her age and they fit perfectly. We got the XXS and she 5'6 97 pounds but these are the kind of jeans that will fit if you fluctuate 5-10 pounds up or down. I was worried that the XXS wouldn't be long enough but these have a 32" inseam. She has a 23" waist in Hollister or A&E. They look skin tight but they're easy to move in, squat, sit, etc. Not bulky or stiff, she can actually do Gymnastics in these. I would prefer a medium/dark wash but other than that they are worth it.

Very slim fitting and flattering but too long These jeans are awesome! They’re very slim fitting, almost like a thick jegging. They’re pretty tight around the ankle too which was important to me. They hit just above my belly button. Material is think which I like and the color is exactly as pictured. I’m 110lbs and 5”3 and I ordered an XS. The waist is a bit tight but definitely fits and the length is too long. If you’re shorter than me even they will be very long. They would probably fit better if they were 1.5 (maybe more?) inches shorter. Also, the ankle would be tighter if I wasn’t short and they’re too long, because I have too much extra material bunched up at the bottom. Other than the length though I love them and they’re pretty flattering!

Drawstring skinny jeans forever!!! I live in a cold region and winter is no joke. I also like to dress cute all year and refuse to wear ugly winter clothes. These drawstring distressed jeans have kept me comfy and in style all winter long without a zipper in site! I have been wearing them with thermals underneath to peek out through the shreds and they are adorable! I get so many compliments on them and when I tell people they are drawstring I get a lot of " I need those!" I don't really see these as high waist as they sit low on my waist a little above my hips. I am 5'3", 190 lbs and these are a little long on me but with a little cuff action they are super cute with any boots and will wear well into the summer months.Do not hesitate to buy these jeans. I guarantee you will be stylish and comfy all day! No muffin top! No bulges!

Stiff material - can’t even pull over my thighs. The material is super thick, awesome!, but it’s so thick and stiff that’s it’s uncomfortable. When putting them on, I had the hardest time pulling them up. When I managed to get them on, I could hardly bend over the material was so uncomfortable and stiff to move in. Also, the legs are wayyyy too long. I’m not that small, I’m 5’4”, but the legs go on for milessss. If you’re someone who doesn’t want to be able to move and you have super long legs, this product is for you. Measurements: 23 waist, 5’4” Stars: 3 (trying to be nice) Would not recommend product.

Great fit for petite curvy women to start, I’m 5’5”, 120lbs, 34-25-38. These jeans fit like a glove honestly. I saw a lot of reviews talking about the material seeming “cheap” and how these AA jeans aren’t like the ones they’ve made in the past. Maybe I was just lucky, but the jeans I got are SUPER thick and heavy. There is NO stretch, so you’ll have to break them in by just wearing them for a few hours. I’m small but pretty curvy and I love the way these jeans fit on me! They compliment my body type just right. Only issues I have are the fact that they have no stretch which makes it super uncomfortable to sit down. I would strongly advise you to size up by 1. When I originally bought a pair back in 2013, I could fit a 24 easily. This time around I decided to go with a 26 knowing my waist size would probably fit a 25. I’m so glad I followed that advice! while the jeans are a little too big in the waist(pic #3), it was nothing a belt couldn’t fix as you can see in the photos, and they flatter me very well. Had I gone smaller I wouldn’t have been able to get them over my hips, they are already snug as is. overall, I’d highly recommend these jeans. They gave me the perfect “mom jean” fit while still accentuating my curves. I’m so happy I took a chance and purchased anyway despite the reviews.

Everything about these pants! I am 5’ and I weigh 97-100 pounds it just depends! These pants around about 1 inch about my belly button and they fit like my Hollister (00) vintage mom jeans. Everyone said go up a size but I didn’t do that and I stayed my usual size which is a size 25. I was honestly thinking of sizing down but the reviews made me second guess everything but if I would’ve dozed down, they would’ve been too tight because these pants have little stretch. My Hollister jeans don’t really fit my waste fully fitted, but I don’t mind any of that because I do like to wear belts. I think if your pant size is your true pant size, for instance you’re a size 26 but you can only fit into a size 26 and not a Size 25, then maybe you should size up because the pants are were thick and aren’t very stretch at all. Also, these pants are VERY long but that was no surprise for me. It took me 6 rolls just to have it fit the leg of my length but you can always trim those down but of course the taller you are, the less amount of rolls you’ll have to do. But other than that, I do like the fit! I’m happy with the sizing I got and won’t be sizing down or up. I really hope this helps you!

Size Up - No stretch Used to own the old pair of these in 28 and read reviews to size up so I sized up to a 29 (my waist is 27.5”-28”). The 29 is slightly too big on the waist but a size smaller would have been so tight around the hips and legs. I doubt I would have been able to sit down. If you owned these jeans in the past do not expect the same pair because the fit in the hips and legs is tighter as I mentioned. These jeans have zero stretch but I knew that coming into this. Overall I love these because they are a true high rise unlike other brands’ claims of “high rise” so I will keep them. Don’t worry about the weird “V” shape in my pictures. This happens with all of my jeans because I have very high set hips and I doubt you will have this problem! I am 5’7” and 128lbs with a short torso.

Size Up - No stretch Used to own the old pair of these in 28 and read reviews to size up so I sized up to a 29 (my waist is 27.5”-28”). Overall I love these because they are a true high rise unlike other brands’ claims of “high rise” so I will keep them. Don’t worry about the weird “V” shape in my pictures. Great pants! I LOVE these jeans. I’ve had the longest struggle (literally years) trying to find a pair of mom jeans that is flattering for my body type. I’m small but have long legs so every pair I find is either too loose and unflattering or just way too short. I like the cropped look but wanted the option to have pants that actually covered my ankle. I’m 5’6 and 110 lbs, and I don’t have super wide hips or a big butt. These hug my hips/butt nicely but are loose enough through the leg. I normally wear a 25, and I ordered both a 25 and 26 since a lot of the reviews say to size up. The 26 fit okay but we’re just a little too loose for my liking on my butt so I opted to keep the 25. They’re a little stiff so they crease a little funky in the front but I think they just need to be broken in and/or washed with a little softener. All in all I’m super happy with these.

Perfect fit!!! LOVE these jeans! I’m 5’8” 134lbs and got a size 6 (27) which is my normal size in jeans and they fit me like a glove. Very glad I didn’t size up! Got the black color and they are a true black with no grey or blue undertones. They are a tiny bit long but i always cuff my jeans anyway no matter the style so that’s not a huge factor for me. They’re actually high waisted too! They cover my belly button which is how high waisted jeans should fit and it’s hard for me to find that because I have long legs. They are pretty stiff and rigid with very little stretch but that’s exactly what I look for in denim. Very thick material so I know they’ll last me a long time. I’m buying two more pairs! Can’t recommend these enough!

Love them. This is one of the first times I’ve ever reviewed an Amazon product. I had to take the time and review it because I love these new jeans! They fit true to size (maybe a touch small) but I really feel like it’s the fact that they are that real denim cotton material and they do not stretch at all that makes them feel like they’re smaller? By far the best thing about the pants for me was the fact that they go all the way down to my ankles. I am 5’10” and do not have a single pair of pants that are long enough for me.

These are THICC jeans, but definitely high-waisted Relevant measurements: 5'2", about 125-130 lb, 26" to 28" waist, 34" hips I usually don't buy jeans for myself, since I find I have a hard time finding jeans that fit me in both the waist and hips -- there's an 8-10 inch difference between them! Nevertheless, I decided that I wanted to splurge and try on some name-brand jeans for once in my life. I ordered the size 27W/32L in Medium Wash. I don't like these jeans. I'll start off with the positives. They are well-constructed, and I doubt they'll fall apart anytime soon. They are definitely the most high-waisted jeans I've ever purchased for myself, which is what I was looking for. And, surprisingly enough, there wasn't a ton of excess fabric bunching up by my ankles -- a problem I commonly have as a 5'2" woman. The color of the jeans is great, and they have an almost "rustic" feel to them, in my opinion. The price is what you'd expect for something by AA. But the negatives, oh boy. The most prominent one: these jeans are THICK and NOT STRETCHY at all. It was a pain trying to get my feet and ankles through the cuffs -- and an even bigger pain trying to get them out. These aren't jeans you can just "slip on" in the morning -- it'll take you a minute to get them on. Then, of course, the thickness -- it means quality, but it also means that I feel constricted when walking and sitting down, to the point where it's actually uncomfortable. Then, even though my waist is a 26", there were still 2-3 inches of gapping between my waist and the jeans. Conclusion: I'm returning these jeans.

Very long, would be great on very tall pear shapes I'm typically an 8 or a 10 because my thighs are somewhat curvy, and read all the reviews and decided on a 30 and two 31s. I'm so sick of stretch in jeans and cropped lengths so was hopeful. Unlike others, I didn't find these to run that crazy small. They are made for a semi-curvy pear shape. My waist is about 30" and my thighs 41". Black 31's were not bad in the waist but big in the thighs, the dark wash 31s were too big, the dark wash 30's I'm keeping. If you have a similar proportion and are sick of waist gaps these are worth considering if the price is right.The denim material itself seems fine but the stitching is not that well done and the thread quality looks poor. I can update when I wear them but I'd thrift actual 90s jeans vs paying over $40 for these. They are not quality wise as good as ones from the 90s but where these differ is they are slimmer in the leg than most 90s jeans and are higher waisted than a number of them as well. I'm tired of companies today calling an 11" rise high. Not for a long torso. These are 13" on the 30's and 31's and actually hit the smallest part of my waist which is the whole point of a high rise!The bummer is they are so long. I wish they looked on me like some of the other reviewers. I'm 5'8" so I'm not short but I did have to roll them twice like the stock photo and it was bulky. Perhaps smaller sizes are shorter or they shrink in the wash? All the ones I bought had an inseam around 33.5". The amount of bulk is going to look bad if you're under 5'6". I might consider hemming them as my preference is not cuffing at all to keep them looking sleek.The black ones in the photo are size 31 in case it helps you on sizing. I feel like they measure exactly what their stated size is when pulled to their max - I.E. the 31's are 15.5" flat and closer to 15" relaxed.

Very nice BUT… These stretchy pants are very comfortable. I love tho blue jeans look. The problem is the pair I received has a noticeable flaw in a prominent place which would make these pants a “factory second” by American manufacturing standards! The flaw is in the topstitching on the outside of the left leg, right at the knee. The machine appears to dave run out of thread and the stitching restarted. This created a one inch section of overlapping top stitching making that part thicker and darker than the rest. The thread ends of the new stitching are even left poking out unsecured. (see photos). This obvious flaw instantly made me disappointed and moved these pants from something I would wear out to dinner to just a pair of pants I will wear around the house. As far as sizing goes, I got a large and I probably should have gotten a medium for a tighter fit. I am 5’6”, 160 lbs with a “fit” build and I find the top of the pants hits me over an inch above my waist—not at or below the waist as pictured on te model. The leg length skims the top of my bare foot, so I would say they run a bit large.

Cute, but SIZE UP!! I ordered this belt in a small, which said it should fit up to a 32" waist. I have a 30" waist so I figured it would be safe, and I was worried a medium would have too much extra belt hanging off me. Well, I was wrong! This isn't even close to fitting. I ended up giving it to my 12 year old daughter who has about a 24" waist and it fits her perfectly, so she'll be using this one. All that said, the belt itself is super cute. Pair it with some summer shorts and you've got a perfect combo for the beach or anytime you want to look summery. Quality seems good. Nice weight to it – doesn't feel cheap or like it will snap anytime soon. Just be sure to order up a size!

They'll do. (They did.) Honestly, I did not expect much from a random brand of jeans. Bought them for a concert. As you can see by the picture, they aren't navy. Really faded color, even before washing. I am 5'2 and generally wear a size 14 short/petite. My butt isn't the biggest, and my legs are skinnier than my waist size. I bought both L and XL. While the large 'fit', they were really snug, so the XL it was. Flattering on my legs, but really long. Had to cuff them a few times over. Can't say they really butt lifted, but they did hug it pretty nicely. Hubs liked them. Will I wear them again? Meh. Would I buy them again for a concert? Probably.

I am leaving my husband to run away with these pants I've run cold all my life, so you'll get my attention with "fleece-lined" pretty much anything. But the h*ck that is my pant-buying life is being 5' 3" with a 29" waist atop 40" hips and rarely finding jeans that fit my butt & thighs but don't gap at the back of the waistband and run completely over the tops of my feet. I got these in Medium and they fit like they were tailored specifically for me. So if you are a curvier body type, these are a really good cut for that. They're long enough on me that I could cuff them, but not so long that I can't just wear them as is. The fabric has plenty of stretch that allows these to fit without being constricting, the fleece lining is SO soft, the denim is a good weight, and I am really really really looking forward to it not being 90°F so when I grab another pair of these in blue they can be the only pants I wear for the next 4 months.

Comfortable and cute I’m 5’4 and weigh 135 pounds and am wearing a small. These pants are so cute and comfortable. This is my second pair. I originally ordered a pair in khaki and liked them so much I ordered another pair. It is a medium weight material, a little too heavy for summer but great for all the other seasons. They wash well and the corners on the pockets don’t curl up and it is minimal wrinkling. I have washed them several times and I put them in the dryer with everything else. The size is true. If I were to translate it to a number size I would say it is a size 6. If you are over 5’5 they might be too short. I love the ties on the bottom to change the style from straight leg to more of a jogger. The only downside is the ties don’t always stay tied so if you are wearing them straight leg style, they can drag on the ground.

Not for me, great fabric. Size up if you have high waist. I decided to try out baggy high waisted cropped jeans for a change. The leopard pattern is great, subtle, still cool. I like the fabric a lot, it has no stretch to it, but it is soft enough, really sturdy, thin, structured. The pants are a bit too cropped for me, but I am tall with long legs (5’9”), so that is expected, and with high heeled pointed boots would look elegant. The problem for me is the top. To look dressy and elegant, these pants should be high-waisted, and they hit at the top of the hips giving an overall boxy shape. For reference, I wear size M (6-8, waist 27” or 28-29” when the pants are mid-rise).

Odd fit These straight leg pants came in a semi translucent garment bag with a product tag. The material is more like cloth slacks than denim. The material is well sewn, the seams even and firm. The hardware is firmly in place and tight with none about to fall off or easily bent. I am 5’9”, 160lbs with a 30 inch waist so according to the sellers sizing chart and the warning the seller provided that the waist runs small, I ordered a size 12 and though these pants fit at the waist nicely, at the hips, waist and groin, they are outright huge. There is enough material for an extra 6 inches of fabric at both hips each and still enough material at the groin that if I was male, these would not be uncomfortable. These pants are plenty long enough, coming to the bottom of my heels. These will be really long on shorter people. The largest issue is how bad these handle washing. The color faded noticeably in a standard cool water wash and medium temp dry. I can not be sure, but the material also feel thinner as well. Priced at $39.99 at the time of this review, these pants just fit to oddly and fade to quickly to be worth the price.

Very soft, and super stretchy denim! NOTE: I don't typically wear shirts tucked in, but for this review I felt I needed to show the fit better. Don't judge too harshly! :) Size Reference: Ordered XXL. I am 5'5", 180 pounds, a 35" waist and 46.5" hips. Weight is carried in my hips and rather large backside. I highly recommend checking the size picture before ordering. I ordered the XXL according to my largest measurement, my hip size. I always find I have a waist gap anyway, so I expected a bit of gap here. Lately I have been struggling more to find pants that fit my hips without highlighting every single fat globule under my skin. If I had realized how stretchy the denim was, I would have ordered an XL. There is so much stretch at the front right at the belly area that it just gathers there. When I sit down I have a big denim poof sticking up. The back has the expected gap, but the thighs fit nicely. There are no belt loops, so there is no cinching the waist in like that. I will likely sew in some darts at the back to help with that. The stretch is so stretchy that I don't even have to unbutton these to take my pants off. I just pulled them right off, no problems. Hopefully giving my measurements and showing pictures of my body proportion will help you as you look to order the right size. I love how soft and stretchy they are, but I definitely needed a smaller size!

I always find I have a waist gap anyway, so I expected a bit of gap here. Lately I have been struggling more to find pants that fit my hips without highlighting every single fat globule under my skin. If I had realized how stretchy the denim was, I would have ordered an XL. There is so much stretch at the front right at the belly area that it just gathers there. When I sit down I have a big denim poof sticking up. The back has the expected gap, but the thighs fit nicely. There are no belt loops, so there is no cinching the waist in like that. I will likely sew in some darts at the back to help with that. The stretch is so stretchy that I don't even have to unbutton these to take my pants off. I just pulled them right off, no problems. Hopefully giving my measurements and showing pictures of my body proportion will help you as you look to order the right size. I love how soft and stretchy they are, but I definitely needed a smaller size!

S/M and L are the same size; extra fabric/gathering above the ankle I ordered the S/M and the L since I'm between a M/L in leggings and don't like a waistband that's too binding. Oddly, when I opened the packaging, they looked exactly the same! I placed one on top of the other, and the rise, inseam, waist, hip, and ankle are identical (see photos of them stacked one on top of the other). I'm pretty sure these are one-size leggings that they've slapped two sizing tags onto because "one-size leggings" sound too good to be true. That said, the L fits me (31" waist, 40" hip) and I had my XXS daughter try on the S/M and they fit her 22" waist without gapping, so there's some magical stretchiness happening here. Length-wise, we're both 5'2" so there's a bit of extra length at the ankle but we can hike them up so it doesn't pool. Pros: - Sturdy exterior that looks like it will hold up to washing, soft fleecy interior (this must be the "velvet" they're referring to as they're NOT velvet on the outside, a bit like a teddy bear on the inside though), very comfy - Not so thick that it makes my legs look bulky, though there's a bit of puffy folding behind my knees and under the bum - Wore outside for an hour at 36F and I can feel the breeze through them but stayed warm - The waist band stays up; no muffin top Cons: - The front and back are identical, so there's not much room for a booty—i.e. the back side takes some coaxing to come up to mid-rise on me, though the front is easily high rise. No issue riding down throughout the day though. - They're oddly jogger-like at the ankle. More pronounced on my daughter since she has skinnier ankles. I tucked the extra fabric into my boot socks, but it's a little odd-looking with exposed ankles. Great for the $13 sale price and we're keeping both pairs.

I ordered an XS Medium Indigo style Levi’s shortalls. I’m 5 feet and weigh 115lbs. I saw most reviews here that this shortalls look good on other women. I decided to order one for myself. I never owned a pair shortalls before. I tried them on. They’re comfortable. It has 5 pockets (2 back pockets, 2 on the sides and by chest area). The top part (the adjustable straps and the waistline) are a good fit for me. However, the shorts itself (both leg openings) were baggy for my taste. They make my butt look flat. Too bad, I can’t ask for a refund. They only have a return or exchange only policy. Since I cannot ask for a refund, I had to salvage this shortalls by folding the hemline twice and secure them with safety pins. So I can sew the folds in place. To make the leg openings somewhat cute and tolerable for me to wear. Hope this helps.

⚠️ORDER YOUR TRUE SIZE!⚠️ Order your true size, don’t size down. Man, I wish I hadn’t listened to the reviews saying to size down because they thought these ran big. They are still wearable and fit like a true small, so I can’t fault the size chart or the garment. These just aren’t going to be as comfy casual on me as they would have been in a medium. I’m still living out my 90s glory days in these regardless. Just trust in Levi’s size chart and order your true size to get the proper vintage fit.

Exactly what I was looking for OK here’s the real deal on this shirt, after reading multiple reviews. I am 5‘7“ and 125 pounds. Someone else my weight/height bought an XS and said it was perfect, and you can see in her pic, her sleeves are too short. I am a true small most of the time (or med.) and this shirt is the right fit in my size. I wanted something more fitted, this shirt has princess seams on the back. Yes it smells a little funny, so wash it! The mid blue is the perfect color for a slightly faded vintage look. The fabric isn’t true denim which it shouldn’t be as a shirt, it’s more like a lighter chambray, but it has the look of denim, which is great for me, I didn’t want it stiff. You can wear it open as a shacket, but it won’t provide much warmth. I wanted to wear it as a shirt. It’s 100% cotton so I expect a little shrinkage, but to me, it would fit even better. [Update: washed/dried, it shrank a bit and fits better! Mostly sleeve and shirttail length.] If you want it more oversized, I would suggest going up as size. I love fashion and can’t wait to style this in endless ways! It’s SO the classic that belongs in every woman’s closet, that’s why they call it wrangler retro!. The pocket placement and size is perfect for us bustier girls.

Not knee length for 5'5" frame. Better suited to taller individuals I was so excited when I received the Belle Poque Denim Skirt. It looked beautiful on the model in the photos. Unfortunately for me, that was not the case. I'm 5'5", and this skirt fell to just above my ankles (see photo). Granted, the model looks like she's wearing this above her naval - I found that if I hiked the skirt up just as high, that I was looking way off proportion. In addition, the sizing is off. I wear a size 12 and have a waist size of 34. I ordered per the sizing chart, which recommended an XL. Unfortunately, the XL skirt fits me pretty loosely - and on top of that, this skirt has a decent amount of stretch in it. I wanted to love this skirt, but to make it fit well - I'd have to take it to a tailor to have it shortened to my knee length. I deducted 1 star for the inaccurate sizing chart and somewhat misleading "knee length" in the title. Despite these issues, the fabric feels nice and soft. One note: after it's washed and dried - you may need to iron over the bottom hem as it likes to flip up.

Not knee length for 5'5" frame. Better suited to taller individuals I was so excited when I received the Belle Poque Denim Skirt. It looked beautiful on the model in the photos. Unfortunately for me, that was not the case. I'm 5'5", and this skirt fell to just above my ankles (see photo). Granted, the model looks like she's wearing this above her naval - I found that if I hiked the skirt up just as high, that I was looking way off proportion. In addition, the sizing is off. I wear a size 12 and have a waist size of 34. I ordered per the sizing chart, which recommended an XL. Unfortunately, the XL skirt fits me pretty loosely - and on top of that, this skirt has a decent amount of stretch in it. I wanted to love this skirt, but to make it fit well - I'd have to take it to a tailor to have it shortened to my knee length. I deducted 1 star for the inaccurate sizing chart and somewhat misleading "knee length" in the title. Despite these issues, the fabric feels nice and soft. One note: after it's washed and dried - you may need to iron over the bottom hem as it likes to flip up.

Very cute skirt I'm 5'8 and 160 lbs and I ordered a large in this skirt based on my hip measurements. I should have ordered a medium. The skirt it too big to wear without a belt and the included belt is too long to cinch properly at the waist. I had to change out with one of my own belts to keep it at my natural waist. With the skirt being on the bigger side and a smaller belt, I ended up with almost a paper bag waist look which isn't bad but wasn't what I was going for. The material is really nice and the quality of the garment is really great. The front pockets are adorable and really flattering. The denim definitely has a stretch to it. If you want this skirt fitted, especially at the waist then I would size down.

For me these shorts fit good. I wouldn’t say they’re super “stretchy”, but they do have some stretch to it. Pleasant Surprise for Midsize person I am 5’8”, approx 170lbs with a 30” waist and 40” hips. I struggle to find pants - jeans specifically - that fit me how I like, and usually don’t have a ton of luck online. I took a chance on these and I’m so glad. Are they perfect? No - I wish the zipper was about 2” longer because when I first went to put them on I thought there was no way they were fitting, but once they were up they fit my waist and have the perfect amount of room through my hips and thighs that I have been looking for. They check a lot of boxes for me, and are even ever-so-slightly long which I never find anymore, and is one of my favorite things to have in loose jeans. I ordered my usual size, 12, again being skeptical, but it’s just right. They fit to my waist without digging in - I had abdominal surgery a year ago and now have a belly pooch that makes most jeans uncomfortable - but I can wear these all day. Also I haven’t had to touch it, but I love that they have the adjustable strap at the waist in back to get just the right fit.

Not for short gals but great pockets I am 5’ 3” and weight 135lbs with an hourglass shape. I ordered an 8 since my waist is about 28 inch and my hips are 39 inch. If you’re short like me, either wear some tall heels or hem the pants. I expect to take off a good 4 inches off of mine. The material is cotton and upon first wear is stiff. I expect them to soften with use. The pants are nicely dyed with good contrast for what is a cow print and come with deep pockets! I can fit my entire hand down the front pockets and the silhouette remains flattering. I think the sizing is true to size just not accounting for petites. The pants take a nice fit to my bottom so that’s a win in my book. The rise of the pants is around the waist which many women like for the tummy control. Overall, would recommend for ladies 5 ft 6in and taller.

Nice jeans, will be even better with some adjustments I’m 5’5,” 165 lbs, my waist measures 36” & my hips are 44.” Per the size guide provided, a 14 would be ideal, but the largest size available is 12. I hoped with a fiber content of 75% cotton & 25% polyester there’d be some stretch - so I ordered the sz 12. Well, these fit, barely (waist-wise). There is zero stretch to these jeans. Although I love the look of these, the waist is definitely snug. Fortunately I sew well, so I have a few tricks up my sleeve. (Or I could lose those last pesky 10lbs). I received these through Amazon Vine & returns/exchanges or not an option. However, even If those options were available to me, I’m not sure I’d return these - the hips & everywhere else (like my bum) fit super nice. I really like the thick cuffs which are super on trend right now, although I’ll most likely sew them securely all around. The cuffs are currently only stitched in place in a few spots (pls see image). These do run long - I’m actually wearing 2.5” wedges in my image. Those deep cuffs could come in handy for anyone who wanted these shortened (or lengthened). I love the style of the front pockets & especially the placement of the back ones. The overall quality of these are excellent. I feel the price of these are fair, especially when compared to one of my fav brands for jeans, Free People. There’s a pair of jeans very similar to these by FP I’ve had my eye on, but they’re $128. I believe with a few tweaks I’ll be very happy with these. I do recommend these jeans but highly suggest following the size guide provided.

Nice jeans, will be even better with some adjustments I’m 5’5,” 165 lbs, my waist measures 36” & my hips are 44.” Per the size guide provided, a 14 would be ideal, but the largest size available is 12. I hoped with a fiber content of 75% cotton & 25% polyester there’d be some stretch - so I ordered the sz 12. Well, these fit, barely (waist-wise). There is zero stretch to these jeans. Although I love the look of these, the waist is definitely snug. Fortunately I sew well, so I have a few tricks up my sleeve. (Or I could lose those last pesky 10lbs). I received these through Amazon Vine & returns/exchanges or not an option. However, even If those options were available to me, I’m not sure I’d return these - the hips & everywhere else (like my bum) fit super nice. UPDATE: Sturdy AF!! LOVE these leggings! I ordered the XXL and was sent 4XL. But I'm glad, because after trying them on, I don't think the XXL would have fit. The 4XL clings but isn't tight which is what I wanted. Upon going back to the product images, I'm kind of wondering how those size models fit into these? I'm 5'3'' 195lbs and I have a pear shape. Measurements hips: 48.9 inches. Waist: 40.9 inches. While taking a picture for review, I noticed a rip on a seam near the hip.. It seems the fabric is doubled so it's not actually a hole. I think I'll just fabric glue it shut and call it a day. UPDATE: I've had these leggings for several months now and they're my go to because of the pocket, high waist and comfortable fit. I just wore them to my trip to NYC and it was wild. While trying to navigate to my seat on the bus as it's moving and being tossed around; the stuff in my pocket got seriously caught on the seat handles not once, not twice, but THREE times and it yanked SO hard I swore my pants were done for! To my surprise I look down and they look absolutely untouched! Still completely in tact! That day I swore I had to update my review and express how sturdy and good quality these are! For that price, what a steal. I'm so happy with these! Get them!! I just ordered 2 more pairs. :D

Love, overall! This are actually sis jeans! I was worried they would be that cheap thin material you often see with stretch jeans but these have a nice thickness! The pockets are amazingly deep. To put it in perspective my fingers are long enough that I can palm a basket ball but if I push my hands down to the bottom of the pockets, the pocket comes up to above my wrist bone. The zipper is nice and sturdy, as is the button. My only thing I wish is that they were about an inch or so longer. I'm almost 5'11 and I think they look a little short on me. Also the crotch on these is long to me, almost like these are means jeans, bit maybe that's just how cargo pants typically fit. Overall, these are very well made and look good. FYI, they look tiny out the package but have a lot of stretch so don't let the put of package look scare you!

Color is insainely DIFFERENT! Sizing off as well I am making this short and (un)sweet..: DONT WASTE YOUR MONEY OR TIME Below are my reasons • THE COLOR DOES NOT EVEN REMOTELY RESEMBLE THE PROVIDED PHOTO OF “MAUVE” as you can tell in my picture I provided these look like totally different leggings, I purchased MAUVE what I thought was a beige/pink blush tinted or light pink leggings, what I received are dark brown(ish) with a purple tint.. it’s not even in the same color category, I’m ok with getting something that is “similar” to the color but this is on a whole other level, and yes.. I even double checked to make sure (1) I ordered the correct color (2) was shipped the item I purchased. I checked the tags, the label on the package and also amazon listing itself ... NOTE TO SELLER: to possibly enhance your reviews maybe update your listing provided pictures to be more accurate on actual color(s) in efforts to please your customer and prevent so many returns. (You can use my picture I provided if you want it’s pretty accurate) • SIZING IS OFF it’s hard to explain what I mean I realize these are One Size.. which means they should have a substantial amount of stretch to them, these do not AT ALL I have tons and tons of OS leggings and all fit fine not matter how big or small I am these are laughable (I am 5ft 174 lbs) they won’t go up the thigh and I have smaller thighs then norm. • THICK FLEECE (not good to wear for spring or summer months) I wouldn’t consider these versatile Type of leggings, they are just way to thick for comfort. SUMMARY: thanks to amazon there is a plethora of leggings options and colors and styles you can choose from, tons of sellers and all are one size (accurately) with rainbow full of colors all hovering the same price, I would stick to another brand in my opinion, just because a listing has over a 1,000 review it doesn’t necessarily mean they are worthy... these may be the worst leggings I have ever purchased unfortunately (and I have 79 pairs of leggings to date)

Non See-Thru Toffee colored leggings & really well made My favorite thing is how well made they seem to be. They look like your everyday leggings with that material ur nails/dry skin snags easily on. However when I flipped the waist down & checked the inside fabric, it was completely fleece lined as promised. I can’t believe what a great deal these are. $10, compared to another company my daughter sent me around the holidays for a gift idea, they were charging $29 + shipping for one pair of fleece lined leggings & only offer in 4 colors. Also super amazing delivery time 2-3days!I’m 5’3 150lb & they fit so amazingly perfect. I typically wear a 12 in Jrs which is the last size b4 being considered a plus size so I got the plus size just to be safe. I am not usually comfortable wearing anything skin tight. It’s not very flattering in my opinion, plus I tend to get overheated easily so the thought of something stuck to me when I’m a sweaty mess is not my idea of comfortable. So I can understand everyone’s obsession with everything leggings/jeggings, they’re so damn easy & u never will have to un-do ur top pant buttons ever again after u eat or feel bloated, they suck u in everywhere! They can be casual, workout gear, dressy with a tightly fitted sweater or blouse, even leggings Under a dress! “What’s not to like?” is my new motto. I don’t think they’re too hot to wear being fleece lined but it’s only 64 in PA. so that’s hard to tell just yet. I am a changed woman & still hold some reserve when & where u wear these,don’t think they should be acceptable funeral attire or for a job interview but with that being said-I plan on buying these in every available color I find , and my favorite part is all the millions of prints & patterns they Have out now. I would absolutely recommend, size is true to description as well as color! I did NOT receive any promotional codes, free clothes/product, browser traffic.

Overall, I like them. I'll start of with the fact that I'm 5'4" with a 29" inseam and about 185 lbs. I carry my weight in my belly and thighs. I purchased these to wear while walking my dogs in the winter. I bought the L\XL size. They're a bit long on me, but that's fine. The fit is great. Not too loose, not too tight. No muffin top. In the photos, I pulled one leg down to the middle of my foot so you get an idea of the length. They are high waisted, roughly 3" above my belly button (I'm touching my belly button in the one photo). The waist is a wide one and I haven't had any problems with it rolling down at all. The material is a little shiny but more knit than one of those slick athletic fabrics. It's a thin fleece too. I think I was expecting more of an athletic legging with thicker fleece. I just walked the dogs. It's 42F outside with a 10mph wind. My legs stayed warm. Hoping these hold up in colder temps. If not, I'll just throw on some silky long johns underneath too. Product was received on time as ordered.

Very long in the legs. I'm keeping them because they are so comfortable I just wish the legs were a better fit.

Seem to be good leggings but might be one size fits all? I ordered a Small/Medium which fit well but the tag says one size so not sure if that means one size fits all? They fit well so I’m happy with my purchase but wondering if there are actually different sizes. For reference I wear a size 8/29 in jeans and am 150 lbs. Also the crotch stretches over to the sides so you can kind of feel it on your thighs or at least that is my experience. Still happy to have fleece lined leggings and they seem like they will be warm.

When I pulled these out of the package I was pleasantly surprised at how soft the material is. I’m 5’9” at 140lbs. They fit perfect. I wore them with my boots. Sad to say right around where my boots hit the fabric pilled. I’m a little disappointed that is why I rated 4 stars instead of 5. However the price is good so I will take my sweater shaver to them to see if I can get the pulling off.

NOT LINED I'm 125 pounds 5'6, and I'm usually a 6 in pants, M for panties. These would have been perfect, if they actually had a lining. To me, if something is lined, that means there are 2 pieces of fabric: the outer layer, and the LINING. These leggings are one piece of stretchy fabric that is satin on one side and fuzzy on the other. These might be good for Texas, but not Michigan winters. I wear leggings to stay warm underneath my full length skirts (yes, even in winter). I chose nude ones because I don't like it when people can see my Halloween leggings when I cross my legs lol. Not sheer at all. I tried these on with black panties underneath and there was no show through. You can see panty lines tho, of course. 5 stars for opacity. The stretch is great. The leggings don't lose shape when worn. Of course, I only tried them on before returning, and didn't test the stretch for a whole day, but it seemed like they would stay good. They were thick enough to be flattering, as I have some butt cellulite and couldn't see it through the leggings. My big hangup is that they aren't lined. It's one piece of fabric that is satiny on one side, and fuzzy on the other. Not true lining. Returning.

Love! But not low rise The first thing I noted about the barrel jeans is the weight of the denim-a true Jean weight/thickness! These are an 80's vintage vibe with the shape, style, and solid 100% cotton material. There's no spandex involved, which was a bit intimidating because I've been used to every Jean having some "give". These fit great despite not having that stretch. The waist is more of a mid rise. I ordered the blue color, not light, not dark, but a standard medium blue shade. Their large traditional Jean button is secure and fits through the bottom hole well. The stitching is solidly done, with no loose strings etc. This is pictured on a model that's 5' 9" tall and about 205lbs. I'm thrilled with these jeans and they are definitely my favorite ones and I will be purchasing more! At 49 dollars, they may not be the least, but they are still a great value.

Comfortable but big I’m 5’0 and at 25 weeks pregnant about 125-130lbs. I have been struggling to find maternity clothes (especially jeans) that are shorty friendly. While these aren’t perfect, they’re by far the best I am yet to find. Really my only complaint is that they are still big. I have to roll them to make the length look good but that doesn’t bother me. They fit well over the tummy, but do occasionally fall down a bit and require me to pull them. These problems weren’t enough for me to return though, as I know will continue to grow and they will fit. Plus they actually are really comfy! Can’t beat the price either so I plan on getting another color.

Love them BUT *Possibly* size down I love these! Very comfortable and I love the elastic panel design. However, I am noticing that they seem to “bag out” fairly quickly and I am thinking I will have to exchange for the smaller size. I am 13 weeks pregnant, but pre-pregnancy, I am a 38” hip and 29-30” waist, 23” thighs. (Side note and pro tip ladies: Measurements are a much more metric than weight, so stop putting your weight in reviews! It’s not because one weight (eg. 140lb.) could look completely different on any two people!!) Anyway, great maternity jeans, just consider sizing down because they will likely stretch out upon wearing and be roomier. *photos from first try-on

Very large and boxy Really can’t decide if I’m going to keep it - I was looking for something a little more modern / trendy, but maybe I’m just middle aged now, haha, because it looks like something my high school students would wear. It feels like an odd fit - really boxy when I’m used to more fitted, and really wide in the body. The sleeves are so long - like to the middle of my thumb. I’m 5’7”, 220 lb, size 16 (bust 44, waist 36) and I ordered XL. I could have sized down which surprised me. Maybe I’d actually like that fit better. My friend LOVES it and says keep, and my husband says it looks like an “art project”. I like that the pearls and rhinestones are attached really well, like shot through the fabric like a permanent earring if that makes sense, not glued or sewn, so they seem like they’ll last. Another reviewer said to wear your hair up or it’ll get stuck - true! Maybe I should keep it to update my boring wardrobe. I think it’ll look great with black denim and a T-shirt, or a simple T-shirt dress. I love that it’s soft denim and not too thick and stiff. Okay, guess I just talked myself into keeping it!

I LOVE THEM, but the flaws are hard to ignore On first try-on I LOVE these jeans! So much! They’re the first jeans that really fit me since pregnancy. But shortly after wearing them, the flaws really started to show: 1- the belly cover is nude/skin color (why??) it makes it so you absolutely have to have a long shirt to cover it, or it looks really weird 2- the jean area barely comes up enough to cover your crotch area. I don’t know why they didn’t bring the jeans line up higher on my stomach, it would have made it easier to wear with normal shirts. And when I sit down, it says low on my bottom too. Not super flattering. 3- they don’t stay up very well. With the stretchy fabric, and the jeans not coming up very high, they just sag a bit. This is definitely not a deal breaker, just a side note. Overall these jeans fit SO good on my legs, and made me feel happy to wear jeans again. I’m currently 140 lbs (120 pre pregnancy) and I’m 6 months pregnant. If you have lots of long shirts, it’s definitely worth it to have nice jeans! I probably would have gone with the dark wash next time, as the stretchy material matches the jean color better. I’m sad that the medium color has a nude stretchy belly, but maybe they can change that in the future.

Small fits me well as a 5'4 / 130 pound preggo (with a big belly) I've been wearing these pants for a couple of weeks now, and while they are a bit firmer than my other Motherhood Maternity skinny pants, they are still pretty comfortable. I wouldn't call these stretchy. For reference, I'm 5'4 and currently weigh probably around 130 pounds. I'm 5 months pregnant but I'm showing as though I were already 7 or 8! Pictures included for reference on fit. I was a little confused when I got these because they are in fact labeled as "Secret Fit Belly" but the actual pants label on the back says 'Branch & Twig' instead of Motherhood Maternity. Not sure if I got the right ones? But based on the photos, they look the same. These fit me well, not too short or not too long. My only slight complain is that at times the top of the tummy can ride down just a tad. But as I have gotten bigger, that has not been such an issue. There seems to be a better hold now. Overall, I think these look really good on me. I love the dark wash color and they have yet to get any sort of piling or lose their color brightness.

I'm 5' 7" 164 lbs. Never find jeans to fit me - anywhere. I have a large waist with slimmer hips. These are buttery soft, with so much stretch that they mould to the body, but also "give," if that makes sense. Having no rigid waistband really helps. Still think they'd be great for smaller girls who could just downsize. They feel great and at last I have some white jeans. At the risk of sounding like a cheesy influencer, I'm definitely buying more.

It is oversized! It has pockets! This is a really fun jacket shirt! I'm 5' 10" about 155 lbs. and am wearing a size L. It is, surprisingly, a bit big on me. The fabric is thick but not "bulky", it's the right balance for a shacket but too much for a shirt. The sleeves are long and roomy. There are usable pockets on both sides (at waist level not just the high front ones). This is a great accent piece, esp. during cool weather providing some warmth but not as much as a jacket.

Thin material but comfortable A previous reviewer said they felt like bathing suit material and that isn’t too far off, in my opinion. They are thin, but not see through, and good for summer weather like a beach or cruise vacation. They are very high waisted but comfortable. Long enough for my 34” inseam legs, as I tend to not wear pants exactly at my hips but close. I bought two pairs, one for work (solid navy), one for home (larger print). I feel like I might be able to go down to a medium for my waist but I like the length in the legs so I’ll keep the large.For reference I’m 5’9”, 34” inseam, 42” hip. Large fit well without feeling tight.

I love these pants I love the feel of the material and they are very stretchy. I am 5'5" and 190 lbs. I first ordered a size 3X and they were too big. I returned them and got a size 2X and they fit better, but I think I could have bought a 1X and they probably would fit better. I feel so comfortable in them and the waist is not tight at all. In that department, they are great. The only thing is the length of the pants. The length is too long as you can see in the pictures. Even when I have them pulled way up, they still go to the floor. Usually, when I buy pants, the length is always just right. To get these off the floor, I have to pull them way up, BUT ... The waistband is so tall that I can first pull them way up and then just gently push the waist to where it should be, and the crotch stays where it belongs too. I really do like the comfort of the pants and I can go anyplace in these not feeling over or underdressed.

What I was looking for! UPDATE: 6/7 - I just received my order for the second pair I bought. Since I loved the first ones ( see original review below) I decided to buy more. I have another pair on the way and I hope they fit like the first. These that I got today are black with blue specks all over them and peachy pink and white flowers all over. Not the cutest but as comfortable as I thought they’d be, I don’t really care too much. Well…..these things were tight! Like I’ll have ZERO room as my belly (and probably hips too) begin to grow more. I’m disappointed. I’m not even going to keep them because they won’t last me even month I’m sure. So I’ll be doing yet another return. We’ll see how the ones that are scheduled to arrive tomorrow will fit. 6/4/22—16 weeks pregnant with twins. I don’t always feel like wearing leggings. I have big legs, big ankles and I just don’t always feel confident enough to wear leggings. Some days I want to throw some baggy pants on other than yoga or sweat pants. They’re a little long but I don’t care too much. They’re thin and the waist doesn’t dig into my stomach at all. They’re pretty loose and airy and I feel like they’ll be great in the heat. They’re not stretchy but I still have room to grow. I’m also looking for something with busy prints so I can let my jiggle, jiggle, and not wear tights and all that. I’ll grab a few more pairs. Oh…I got a 3X and they’re still loose but I’m Ok with that. I’m about 230 lb, 5’3”.

Meh. I'm not in love with it. The fit is a little weird. Im 5'3 and fluctuate between 128-135. I got the 121 Little flower in a small. Material: The fabric isn't as stretchy and the feel is not flowy or soft. You definately feel the synthetic polyester against your skin. Since its a bit stiff you can't really move or do a lunge comfortably without feeling strain. The Cut: The top half is about 11.5 in. from crotch to waistband. I like high waist bottoms but if you have a short torso I wouldnt recommend it. Sure you can fold it over but the top garter which is very tapered has no give and it will bite into any soft spots in your tummy area. Any bulges the fabric will stretch and thin showing a lighter slightly see through spot. The pant leg is very long. 29" inseam and looks more like a straight pant leg than a bell bottom flow. Sure you can hem it, but you will lose any slight bell bottom shape. Design: The print is beautiful which is why I gave it more stars than I would have otherwise. The color is bright and crisp an no bleed throughs. My only gripe is that the print is not flattering to my butt. Im not sure if this makes sense but the print has a more of a camouflage effect to make things bumps appear smaller. This might also have something to do with the cut falling straight down from the hip instead of your thigh like a bell bottom should. I think even if I size up the cut will still be wierd and any hemming I can do will just make the pants boxier. I want to give it a shot because of the pretty print but its just not what I was looking for so I will be return it.

Kinda cheap and not necessarily stretchy These were cute, but not quite as expected compared to other leggings/stretchy pants I’ve ordered. The material was pretty thin and cheap feeling/looking. I am 5’8”, 140lbs and ordered a M, which is usually on point. These were a little too big and the top of the waistband felt like it didn’t give. The length was actually really nice because I can never find pants long enough, but the waist and legs were a bit too baggy. I probably could size down to a S, but am not because of the material and waistband (a small might be too small at the top and not give). I also wasn’t expecting the inside to be white material on a mostly black pair. Returned without getting a new one.

I need these pants in every color and print!!! I literally have NOTHING bad to say about these pants. The material is soooo buttery silky! The top of the pants rises to just above my belly button. I can even pull them higher to make the leg length shorter so I can wear flats with them. Ok, so for reference: I’m 36 years old, bra size is about a 40DDD or E (not really sure; I don’t even wear bras anymore), 5’6” and weigh about 290lbs. I ordered the XXXL. It fits beautifully. It’s not snug in the waist so I probably could’ve gone down a size if I wanted a tighter fit. I wear camis with built in bras that also help to tighten and flatten my tummy so that’s probably another reason why these fit so good in my waist. I’m very curvy and have some nice size hips and these pants just flatter all of it! I love the loose legs. You can dress them up or down (apparently they’re called pajama pants???). I apologize for the picture. My full length mirror is in my daughter’s playroom. I got the purple pants and paired it with one of my yellow tops. This is now my go-to outfit for all LSU games! GEAUX TIGERS 🐯 I just can’t express how much I love these pants. I’m ordering another pair as soon as I’m done writing this review!!

Lightweight and flowy - great for summer Size small 5’5”, 146-150, 32DDD, These feel like loose fit pajama pants, so if you want a heavyweight fabric skip them. I like the lightweight feel, especially for summer. They will definitely show panty lines so thong or commando required. They’re ankle length on me so I’ll wear flats, but the waist is very elasticized and would be easy to wear a couple inches lower if you want to pair with heels. They’re high waisted (sit about an inch above my bellybutton) so good for crop tops too. But if you’re looking for tummy control or thick fabric they won’t be for you.

Cute and Eye-catching I have mixed feelings about these pants. They LOOK CUTE and they are super soft. I think I was expecting them to be slightly less loose in the thigh area or maybe wider at the bottom for a more flared leg look? It seems to be the same width all the way down. . That being said, they are definitely long enough. I am 5'7" and these hit me about an inch above my belly button and touch the floor. I'm not sure if they will shrink or not so I'd plan on washing them cold water and hang dry for good measure. . They are also VERY lightweight. Great for summer or tropical weather. If you plan on wearing these in the winter time in places where it can get 30 or below farenheight, you will be VERY cold. Basically don't go outside. . I think these will do well for my vacay and for work too. I think I could dress these pants up with heels and the right blouse or blazer for a relaxed office look. . I am giving 4/5 stars only because of the width of the pant and because I feel the pattern was much more subtle in the photos whereas in person it's much more eye-catching. I was wanting a more subdued look.

I think these will do well for my vacay and for work too. I think I could dress these pants up with heels and the right blouse or blazer for a relaxed office look. . I am giving 4/5 stars only because of the width of the pant and because I feel the pattern was much more subtle in the photos whereas in person it's much more eye-catching. I was wanting a more subdued look.

Skip if you’re 5’2” or less These would be great lounge pants. I’m not sure I could pass them off as work pants due to the shiny yoga pant material but that would depend on where you work. In a professional environment? These aren’t the pants you’re looking for. Additionally if you are 5’2” or below like me, the inseam will likely be too long. After reading reviews from people who were my height or less, I thought I would be able to wear these with flats to do school pickups or run quick errands. The pants were so long! Even with three inch heels, my pants caught on my feet. I do have small size 5 feet so the pant legs totally cover my foot. Since the inseam measurements in reviews and answers are all over, I think they must not be consistent. On the pair I received, the inseam was 32” and overall length was 43.5” which is just a tad longer than the description. My recommended size was a large but based on reviews, I ordered a medium and they still felt large. I’m undecided if I want to try the small, I doubt they would fit into my 42” hips comfortably. 🤣

Shockingly tall enough!!! Odd to refer to a pair of pants as life-changing I know but I am 6 ft tall and most of that is all legs & as a result I stopped wearing pants years ago because it just wasn't worth it to try to find ones that fit me right. I literally just don't wear them. Whenever I have found pants that are long enough they fit all wrong everywhere else and pants that fit in the waist and butt are always at least 4 inches too short. These are honestly the first pants that have fit correctly and that I have worn in many years. They're stretchy and comfortable and plenty long. I don't know how shorter people would with these pants as again I am 6 feet tall and they fit me perfectly but I couldn't be happier. The only reason I bought them is because I received a different pair of the same brand as a gift and could not believe how perfectly they fit and since then I have purchased them in five different patterns and will continue to buy more!! If you have extremely long legs and have a hard time finding pants that fit, but these now!! Game changer for us tallies!

Great! Like the other reviews said, very lightweight stretch poly material but not see-through. I'm 5'8", 178lbs and got the XL which fits me perfectly, snug enough to stay put with a smooth look and no sagging in the crotch but not squeezing me too tight around the waist. Waistband is just above belly button at natural waist. Because of the thinness of the material I will likely wear these with a thong to avoid panty lines but, again, they're totally opaque. I have a suspicion that this material might pill eventually but i just got them so time will tell. Exactly the right length on me to fall just above the floor- great for flats or if I want to show off heels. Super-comfortable and very nice-looking pants for the warmer months. Very happy with the purchase.

Taller lady approved! I was skeptical of these pants, since most in this style are too short on me. I generally look for a 33 inch inseam on my pants. These surprised me. They fit as expected. I’m 5’7 plus a little (I used to be 5’8” but I have scoliosis) and 205 lbs. I normally wear 14/16 and I ordered an XL. I don’t wear heels often (prefer flats) but the third picture is me on my tippy toes. They allow for heels if you bring them down slightly up top. I also appreciate the placement of the second seam up top. It doesn’t split the belly like some pants do. I already ordered a second pair and I might order more. If you’re a taller lady that gets discouraged over the inseams, give these pants a chance. :-) Shorter ladies will likely need to hem them.

Stretchy and comfortable These faux leather shorts are cute and fit well. They are pretty stretchy all over (including the waist), so they'll easily accommodate different shaped bodies. They seem well made (seams seem strong) and the zipper runs smoothly. The outside 'leather' is a little shiny (iridescent (?)) looking with a bit of a woven fabric pattern (it is not a smooth looking finish). I took an up close pic of the pocket area so you can see the material's finish. I like the way the material looks. The inside of the shorts is the backside of the 'leather' and it's a soft polyester/nylon feeling material, so the 'leather' isn't touching your skin, but the shorts are not lined with a separate material. I took a pic that I hope shows what I'm talking about.I ordered a size L (my normal size) based on their size chart and they fit comfortably (I have a 32" waist and 40" hips). They fit true to size. If you are in between sizes and want a comfortable fit, I would say size up and if you want a more skin tight fit, then size down. The tag says you can hand wash them or wash them inside out on the delicate cycle. They came pretty wrinkled (I haven't washed them yet and the pics show the way they arrived), so I think I'll need to wash and gently steam these to smooth them out. These are fun shorts that will go with a variety of tops and shoes (or knee high boots).luvamia Womens Shorts Trendy High Waisted Faux Leather Look Stretchy Jean Shorts Short Denim Pants with Pockets Comfy (Black, L)

Cute, trendy, flattering fit So far I am loving these shorts. They are lightweight, not bulky material, but not so thin that they feel cheap or show underwear lines. I am 5'2" 128 lbs, waist 29" and hips 36". I got size medium and could probably have gone with small for a more fitted pair, but based on the size chart I was a combination of small and medium measurements for waist and hips and didn't want to take the chance on it being too small/unable to zip up. Medium does not look too loose or baggy on me, but the fit is definitely looser than how many ppl would wear this style and material. Based on how they fit me and the stretchy give to the material, medium would definitely still fit someone a few pounds/several inches bigger. I do like the stretchiness to the material, but it makes me wonder if they would stretch too much after sitting in them/wearing them for an extended period of time. I haven't worn them yet except to try on, so will update this review should any of those issues occur. But so far, I'm a fan and will probably buy in more colors!

My favorite belt I work in a saw mill and have lost over 60 pounds in the last 2 years and it's been so uncomfortable trying to find belts I don't have to fight with or belts I don't break within the first months time. With me being a female suspenders just don't work for me. I started using Bungie cords to try and keep my pants up while I lift logs all day long and after more weight lose they werent working for me either. My boss told me about adjustable belts and options outside normal belts you can buy at Walmart. So...I found these belts here on Amazon and ordered them. I wore one all week last week and love it. Depending on which pants I wear and how high up and low they fit on my waist will depend on if I have to wear the metal piece that comes along with the belts. Most times with me wearing my pants below my belly button I can hook these belts to the front belt loops and won't even have to use the piece of metal to hook the belt at the zipper line of my jeans. I love not having to fight a belt or constantly pulling up my pants or trying to constantly make adjustments just to work and keep my pants where they need to be. I would definitely buy these belts again. I'm a 29 inch waist and I tightened the belt as tight as possible and it keeps my pants up and keeps it comfortable. I don't have to worry about my pants dropping. 🤣 I would definitely buy these belts again.
