Very nice pants for Fall/Winter These are such nice, corduroy pants! I got them for my granddaughter, age 15, who really liked the look of them on Amazon. She’s wearing an adult size 8-10, and is very picky about what she likes to wear for school. Meaning, they had to be black. Haha. Seriously, though, black is her signature color, as she proudly proclaims. She loves that these are cargo pants, and also that they’re corduroy, rather than plain old denim. The corduroy is thin-wale, and the fabric is very soft, making it a perfect, cozy feel for Fall/Winter. The pants fit her perfectly, too. The fabric has a nice bit of stretch in the waist, but not obviously so - it’s just enough to stretch over the hips, then fit her perfectly in the waist. The length is even great! So many pants are too long on even average-size women/girls. These are a tiny bit long on my 5’4” granddaughter, but she prefers them to be long and slouchy. She’s a tough sell these days, but she is honestly delighted with these pants. The price of $34.99, as of this writing, is very reasonable for a quality-made pair of pants. These are machine wash and dry, perfect for a busy family with 3 kids. I’m very pleased with these pants.

The fabric has a nice bit of stretch in the waist, but not obviously so - it’s just enough to stretch over the hips, then fit her perfectly in the waist. The length is even great! So many pants are too long on even average-size women/girls. These are a tiny bit long on my 5’4” granddaughter, but she prefers them to be long and slouchy. She’s a tough sell these days, but she is honestly delighted with these pants. The price of $34.99, as of this writing, is very reasonable for a quality-made pair of pants. These are machine wash and dry, perfect for a busy family with 3 kids. I’m very pleased with these pants.

Has a TON of stretch & are ok, but longer would be nicer! Ok, well...since there were no reviews, I wasn't sure about these, but thought I'd give them a try! I have to say I was surprised with how comfy they are! VERY Stretchy, so they are VEEEEERY forgiving size wise just wished they were a wee LONGER!! I went with their measurements & this is a Large! For reference I'm apx 5'7" & 135lbs with an apx 29-30" waist & 40ish" hips. You can see how MUCH it stretches at the waist in the pics! That does make them feel nice on though!! I would have sent them back & went with a med, but I am afraid that would be too short then, so I'm mulling over just sending them back?! Haven't decided yet though!? They seem well enough made, & at prices as they are, they seems reasonable I suppose?! I like the high waist & button fly! Hubby liked them, so heck, might keep them!? LOL They ARE comfy! They'd be great fishing jeans for sure as they give when you bend & move!! They do have a strange odor though right out of the package, that I HOPE will go away after washing! Kind of a petroleum type smell?!? NOT pleasant at all though, but maybe due to the hot weather & being in plastic? But, I'm sure washing will help! All & all they are nice & would recommend. I'd say the sizing is pretty much spot on, so pay attention to that & you should be fine! Hope this helps! :-) Thanks much!

Great! Runs large by 2 sizes. Generous Length (but I'm short). I'm 5' 3.25" and around 130 lbs. I usually wear a size 8 in women's pants. I have worn and loved Carhartt work pants which I bought in a size 8, but they ran a size larger and give after wear. These Dickies are a great, more reasonably priced work pants very similar to Carhartts. Lots of pockets and a little stretch with the durable material. The size 6 are good but big. I feel like I could have went to a size 4 for a "beltless," fit. I plan on wearing a belt so they don't fall down too much especially when it's been a couple wears before washing/drying. Depending on how much room you like in your work pants, I highly recommend going a size down for some wiggle room, 2 sizes down to go beltless and have it fit more like a glove at the waist. I have a 30" inseam and they are long! I'll have to hem them especially since they'll sink down after some wear and pocket weight.

Bad pockets, Needs some design improvements Edit #2. I took another chance and ordered a second pair in green and received another with skimpy fabric at the pocket and showing white pocket liner. I’m so disappointed in Dickies. I really like these pants and the pair that is made correctly is great but the stingy fabric around the pockets of the others is a deal killer for me. The difference between the fabric depth into the pocket on the “good” pair is an extra half inch. So congrats Dickies, you saved 5 cents on fabric and completely lost a customer. Edit: The second pair of pants I received in green do not have the pocket lining issue. Both pairs stretch out as you wear them and are a bit too big and long despite sizing down. I am 5’5.” Definitely going to need a belt with these but at least they give good freedom of movement. Fit is large, I went with a size 4 reg thinking they might be a touch snug but they are pretty loose and feel like they will loosen up more than I would like with wear. I’m a little “fluffier” than normal right now and if I drop any weight they are definitely going to be too loose. What I really don’t appreciate is the stinginess on the pocket fabric. They did not put enough of the heavy weight twill in the pocket opening so despite being a little on the big side as you wear the pants the lighter bright white fabric shows. It’s looks terrible and only happens on one side. I ordered two pairs and might send the second pair back. The first pair unfortunately I have already worn.

Solid, sturdy work pants sized for women So far, so good. I'm in between an 8 and a 10 so I ordered both. The 10s were gigantic and even the 8s are big. I probably could have gone down to a 6, but I washed these once and they shrank a little, maybe more next time as they're like 97% cotton. Even though they're big, with a belt they hold up comfortably and don't get the chafe-y, baggy crotch you get with some pants. They are also wide leg and very long. I cuffed them, but I'll probably have to hem them so the cuffs don't get full of sand. That being said, they're still GREAT solid, sturdy work pants. They're nice and thick duck with a double front and just enough stretch to be comfy. It's so hard to find quality work wear for women, so I would definitely recommend these pants and would buy them again, but in a smaller size. For reference, I'm 5'7", 145 lbs.

Great, considering the style This is a great relaxed “boyfriend” style overall. For all the reviews saying it’s saggy in the crotch or makes your butt look bad, I’m sorry, but that’s just the nature of overalls... especially a looser fit. And especially for those of us who grew up in the late 80s early 90s who are conditioned to think tight pants are the only pants worth wearing in public. I’m generally between a small and medium in both pants and tops. 5’6” with long legs but a short torso. I weigh 145, but am an athletic build so built about the way someone who weighs 130-135 who doesn’t lift heavy weights. I ordered a medium. The best thing you can do before returning these is take some selfies standing how you normally would for a picture and send to some of your GIRL friends. We’re usually our own worse critics, and if you’re not used to overalls or this particular fit, a second opinion helps. Don’t ask a man, they’d be happiest to see you with no pants ever. Anything that leaves your shape to the imagination to any degree annoys them 😂 They are soft and comfortable, a thinner stretchy denim. The light color is great for summer. I only gave 4 stars because out of the wash, I don’t like the way the top seam of the chest pocket sort of puckers and folds over. Easily fixable by wetting it down and ironing it(I use my straightener 😂), but annoying. Regardless, I will be buying these in the distressed style as well.

No Fancy Box Here…. This belt came in a clear plastic bag. Very cheap presentation. The belt is also way too big. I’m a 29” waist so I decided to go up to a Medium to be safe and the belt’s smallest loophole doesn’t fit me. Huge. The quality is nice, feels like leather and the belt is pretty, but honestly, I didn’t appreciate it coming to me secondhand; even the tag had been removed from the belt. Someone must have returned it without the box so the company threw me this. Not happy with that. At least give me a discount if I'm buying a used product.

Measurement and comparison of "small" and "medium" sizes My waist measurement (smallest point where I might wear a belt) is 31", which is at the top of the small size and low in the medium size, so I ordered both to compare. The small size is about 38" total length (not counting the buckle), and the medium is 43" inches long. The small belt definitely fits my small waist measure; I would be using a hole in the middle of the range. However, it would be way too small to fit lower on my hips, where I sometimes wear a belt (my hip measure increases to 41" ). The medium size belt is a touch too large for my 31" waist (shown in the photo) -- I will have to punch another hole. However, I will keep this one so that I can wear it lower on my hips as well. So my advice for sizing is to take into account where you wear your belt, and if you're near the ends of the measurement ranges, size up for versatility! Too big is better than too small since you can punch another hole, or I guess shorten the whole belt by removing the buckle.

What a bummer!! I was so excited to get these shorts! Read lots of reviews and loved the look of these. But...PROS: The initial fit was amazing! Put them on and they fit perfectly from the start. Love the 3 1/2 inch inseam, not too short, not too long. Love the functioning flap pockets in the back. I truly thought I'd found my perfect summer shorts and would be returning to purchase more colors. However...CONS: The spandex... Unreal! What did fit absolutely perfectly were pretty much falling off of me after about an hour of wear. Too much spandex? I don't know. They are 96% cotton and 4% spandex? But they no longer fit perfectly.I'm about 5' 6" and 135 pounds, I wear size 6 in bottoms for pretty much everything. I bought these in a size 7. These things were SO sloppy loose after wearing a very short time, I could hardly keep them up. I literally can put both arms down inside the sides of the shorts along my hips and I still have room. I can take them down to my ankles without unbuttoning them or even trying to get them over my hips. Such a disappointment. I don't know. The second pic is how they end up just sitting on my hips, sagging. Even though I've worn them, I'm thinking I'll return them... Ugh.If you're considering these I'd either reconsider or size down a size...

As I (begrudgingly) get older, I struggle with trying to remain fashionable, but also dress my age. Shorts can be tough for me because I already don't love wearing them and there are so many super short, uncomfortable pairs that seem to let everything hang out. I took a gamble on these as I wasn't exactly sure on my size or how they would look. I'm 5'3 and 100 lbs, and usually wear either 25 or 26 in jeans and I opted for the size 2s in these. They ended up being absolutely perfect! They fit me super well- they have a fair amount of stretch, but they're not too snug around my waist and are incredibly comfortable. I also love the length of them. They're not super long (a look I don't love), but are a "respectable" length. I feel like they're trendy, but I also feel comfortable going out and about in these and that I look my age (almost 40), but a cool version of it. I love the wash and it will go with so many different things. It's easy to tuck shirts into the shorts or leave them out. The price on these is great and I'll definitely be getting a lot of wear out of them this summer!

So much cheek I ordered these in my usual size 4 (27 waist) and while the high rise is really cute, the wide leg is too revealing for my 39 year old self. NOT a no gap waist for very curvy Unfortunately, NOT a curvy enough fit and the “no gap waist” did nothing but gape! Really liking them so far These jeans are nice and stretchy. In Levi's Mid Rise Skinny jeans, I have been wearing a 10, but they are cutting me in half at the waist lately. I am about 5'4", 143 lbs. I carry my weight in my stomach, so that's where most of my "curvy" is. I ordered a 10 in these jeans, hoping the extra stretch would help in the waist. They were, indeed, very comfortable in the waist, which measured 16" flat across. But the thighs and front were kind of baggy. I sent them back and ordered two pairs of 8s and they fit much better. The waist is a bit smaller, measuring 15" across, but there is so much stretch available that it's not a problem. The butt and legs fit closer, although they are not what I would consider tight, as you can see by my picture. Inseam on the size 10 and the 8 is 30". And they are definitely high waisted on me, not mid. Maybe I'm short waisted, but they come up to just above my belly button. Kind of strange feeling at first, but even though I do not tuck my shirts in, my muffin top always wanted to show through my tops. That isn't happening as much now, so I'm liking it. The size 10 pair were the Bristol-Whiskers, a kind of light blue. The 8s I ordered are Salida and a really dark pair, Parker Whisker. Out of the 3, the Salida are my favorite. A medium blue (lighter than they appear in the picture, but darker than the 10s). The other 8s, the Parker Whisker, are extremely dark blue and I may exchange them for another Salida. The "whiskering" on all of these jeans is pretty mild, much less than it looks in the pics they show. Today is the first day of me wearing my new jeans, so I can't tell if they will bag out, but they don't seem like they will.

Perfect!! I'm 5'3" 160-165lbs, slightly muscular body composition. And I love this dress. My arms are larger in my biceps and this doesn't make my arms feel too fat etc. I really love this dress. I want to wear it everyday. So comfortable.

Cute country style! I ordered this dress to wear to a country music event. I have some tan boots that I adore, and I think this will pair perfectly with them along with my custom, cowboy hat and wrangler backpack. The ruffles on the sleeves are cute, but still feminine. The length of the dress is excellent, I didn’t want something too short. The denim is durable and does not feel cheaply made at all. It feels like it will last through a few washes as good denim should. I chose the lighter color as I knew I would be outside and didn’t want something too dark. I’m not a fan of the belt, so I’m probably going to change that out. It wraps around my waist and just dangles in the back. I’m 5‘6“ tall and my measurements are 37 inch bust 31 inch waist and 44 inch hip. I’m between a medium and large, but I decided to go with the large due to my hip size. It did fit true to size. Overall and very happy with it and I can’t wait to put my full ensemble together for the event!

Usable Pockets! This cute dress caught my eye because of the pockets. Most of the time, women's pockets disappoint me because they're either tiny, or they're so stretchy that anything with a little weight ruins the look. Not this time.The pockets on this dress are huge! I tried to find something big to illustrate the size because my phone just disappears into the depths. The hairbrush in the photo is of standard size. That should give you some idea. Because the dress is made of denim, there's no stretching problem either. I'm 5' 4" and 115 pounds. I don't know why I decided to order a medium, but that's what I did. I can make it work, but I'd get a small if I ordered again. These dresses seem to run true to size, but the belt gives a little leeway for those in between sizes. Actually, I'll probably change or modify the belt. For some reason it was made super long. It's as if it was meant to be a tying belt, and the buckle was added as an afterthought.

Not as expected First off, the description said machine washable, the label says simply one word “ Hand”. Doesn’t say what the material is on the label. But it’s very rough, the last pair of pants I had like that, snagged on everything. Also the zipper is very hard to zip, I think it’s the wrong size because it bulges, they also seem bigger than a small, I’m going to wash them in the machine to see if they will fit better. The price was good the pictures looked good, but………..I’m 5/4 and weigh 108 if you want to compare. I guess they’ll be ok to hang around the house, but I doubt I’ll be wearing these in public unless they wash up good.

Cute - size issues maybe for different jeans and body types Size ordered: X-Small, at least according to the tag Size normally wear: around a 4 in most jeans, or anywhere from a 25-27 according to jeans makers Body Type: Fit, 116 to 117 pounds, 5’2”, relatively long legs compared to top of body (I am not a petite length in pants), small waist, curvy. There is a LOT of different reviews on the sizing of these jeans, so first off think about the fit you want. If you want something a little loose and comfy—not at all tight but not baggy—then go for your size or one size down and that will vary according to how you are shaped. For example, I chose x-small because I read a review from someone who seemed to have my body type and was my exactly weight and near-exactly my height and she ordered an x-small and said they fit loose on her. Good thing I did because the small I almost bought would have been way too big. I wanted something comfortable—not baggy or ‘relaxed’ fit but not super tight, something different from all the tight skinny jeans I have. Once you select a size, try them on, and if you don’t like the fit, return them for another size. Really, that’s the only way because I think these jeans’ fits are all over the place, and according to your body type, they will fit one person one way and another person another way! Or maybe pairs are sized wrong from the get-go! Length wise too. Length: my legs are relatively longer for my height and these are long enough on me to wear heels—maybe 3-4 inch heels. I have on wedges in the sample photo I took but I wore 3 1/2" heel boots with them the last time I wore them. I probably wouldn’t wear flats with these. Mine fit loose in the waist but I have a small waist so I expect that with jeans. These come to just under my belly button, which is where I like jeans to be if they are high-waisted (these are). I have to belt these or else I'm pulling them up. However, everywhere else they fit me great. Not tight but fitted to my body. There is some stretch to them. They are almost as soft as pajamas. Really soft and comfortable. I didn’t buy them for some retro look or nostalgia, I just wanted something different—way too many skinny jeans in my closet. These are super cute, fun, and something I don’t have in my closet. A negative: They stink. Yeah. Sort of an old ‘mildew’ type odor. I had to wash them twice and I’m still not so sure it’s all completely out. I used a mixture of water and ammonia the second wash and rinsed them twice and that helped a lot. I could wear these for hours and be comfortable. I don’t know how long they will last as they are a bit on the thin side but for this price even if they don’t last, I won’t have wasted my money. Just super cute, super comfortable, and not at all what I expected as I have not had good luck lately buying jeans. I’d get another pair and wish they had these exact jeans with some other kinds of designs.

Super Cute.... Awesome 70's vibe. I am about 5'5", 128 lb.. but very hip-py. I ordered the small, and I find them a little more snug than I prefer. So I am going to try a medium. They seem a little tight in the hips, and they're oddly tight at the knees. They're a little higher rise than I would prefer. I firmly prefer low-rise pants. These come right up underneath my belly button. Probably if they weren't so high-rise I would keep the small. They are very stretchy. I feel like I have short legs for my body, and with my shoes on, the length comes to about a 1/2 inch from the ground. My inseam is 30 inch. UPDATE: I ordered the medium and they were just too big. They fit better in the hips and the waist, but they were oddly baggy in the crotch. It looked like I was trying to smuggle the crown jewels in there. Also the inseam was about two to three inches longer on the medium. They were dragging on the floor. These are really super cute pants, but sadly I'm going to have to send both sizes back. I wish they had numbered sizes.

Runs small. And extremely short The embellishments on these jeans is quite beautiful. The embroidery looks of good quality., It appears to be sewn well. And the look is absolutely adorable. I’m not so sure about the button on the front which sounds a little “tinny.” I half expect it to fall off and will keep an eye on it. Do be sure you check out the detail on the bottom of the pants. They are very vintage looking and beautiful in my humble opinion, but it almost looks like an old pair of vintage pants for someone very very short, with the hem rolled out. The bottom is much darker, suggesting that the cuff did not fade like the rest of these well worn jeans. And the edges are frayed. It is exactly the beautiful vintage look I was going for, but it may not be for you, so do check out the pictures. I took the advice of other reviewers and ordered a size up. My stats 5’9”, 155 pounds, usually size medium or size 8 in US. I ordered a large and it fit fine. The waistline, tummy, hips, thighs are all good. It fits snug (not tight) and has a bit of stretch available in case these shrink or I gain a few pounds. The medium would definitely have been too tight. The backside looks nice, and again, the embellishments are beautiful. But the length is much shorter than expected. These land mid ankle, just above my foot, where my skinny jeans land. Not the right length for a boho vintage look. And I would certainly wear these with tall shoes like funky boots or 70s inspired sandals, so the length Is a big issue. I’m struggling with whether to return or keep, as they are gorgeous and I’m pretty crafty. I plan to take these on a field trip to the fabric store to find something to lengthen the bottom ... white lace, tulle, eyelet to match the embroidery? Or maybe navy/denim to blend with the bottom? If I find the right embellishment, I can make these even more beautiful. If not, I’ll need to return, which breaks my heart. But these are just too short to wear as is. Update: I added this 3 1/2” navy blue lace trim (cost $8, 1 hour hand-sewing) to lengthen the jeans. I absolutely love the results, and now these are unique and mine. So don’t fret, tall gals. A little creativity can make these short jeans just right for your height!

Pants for short people. Advertised as "extra tall" w/ only a 32" inseam I'm 5'8" and 145lbs, I usually wear a size 2-4. I bought the size "small extra tall" thinking that I'd be able to wear them with heels. I was weary since there wasn't any information on how long "extra tall" actually was, but I figured that it would be the same fit as the pants the model is wearing in the product image. On closer inspection, it appears that they photoshopped out the white sneakers that the model was actually wearing and added in the black heels to make the pants look like they come down farther. According to the size chart, a small is 38.5" long (length of inseam isn't listed). My pair is exactly 40" long with a 32" inseam. The design is pretty and the pants are comfortable, but they are just WAY TOO SHORT!

Runs a little big, but I love them!!! I love these embroidered jeans!!! Height 5’2”, 120 lbs., waist is 29”, hips 35”. Gorgeous! These are for my 12 year old. She is almost 5'1" and very slender (87lbs) with pre-teen hips. These are so freaking adorable on her! They're definitely a bit loose in the waist so she's tied a cloth belt on as you can see in the pictures. They're also a bit loose in the hips, but it looks fine, especially for a 12 year old. They fit pretty close to the skin through the thighs, but not tight. The jeans seem to have a good amount of stretch so I expect she'll be able to wear them at least a couple years as she grows into them. They'll certainly work well for someone larger than she is depending on how snug you like them Two things to know: As you can see in the pictures, the length is pretty good on her. Other reviews saying these run short are why I decided they might work for her, and it's true. She's wearing shoes with a heel, but they're still not that long on her. You could have legs a little longer, but if you're fairly tall these likely won't hang as low as you want. It looks exactly like the hem wasn't sewn which I thought was a flaw at first. It looked cute that way so it didn't matter, but you can't clearly see how it looks like you can turn the bottom under twice for the hem, or even looks a bit like it was turned under but not stitched. Looking at the Amazon photos again made me realize they're actually designed that way. They'll probably develop a bit of a cute frayed edge. Just look closely at those pictures and be sure that's the hem you want... It stitch it up yourself, it would be easy to do.

The high-waisted design is exactly like the pictures, and I’m really happy with the length. I went for the light blue color and ordered a size 2 based on the size chart. The fabric is a nice blend of cotton, polyester, viscose, and spandex, which adds to the comfort. However, the fit is a bit loose around my waist. For reference, I’m 5'3", 106 lbs, and my waist is 26 inches. Despite the slight sizing issue, I’m not too concerned since I love the unique gradient of the jeans. They also come with front and rear pockets, which is a nice touch. Overall, while the loose fit isn’t the most flattering for my frame, the jeans are still stylish and different. I’d give them a solid 4 out of 5 stars.

Stretchy high waisted tall jeans Can I get an amen for the TALL jeans!!!!! I love these! I am 6’1 and it’s soooo hard to find jeans that are long enough and are still flattering in all the right places, but these are amazing! They actually fit in both length and waist. These are beyond comfortable and very stretchy, these are jeans that you could wear all day and never be uncomfortable. These are true to size.

Cute, flattering, fit close to expected. Know your measurements. I read a lot of other reviews before purchasing, mostly about fit. I have an hourglass shaped build at 5'2 and 190-200 lbs. The fabric is soft and not super stiff like denim can sometimes be. The cut of the skirt is super flattering, even if you have a belly. I could see this looking especially nice on a top heavy or straight framed body type due to how it flares from the waist. The belt is of a nice quality and the "metal" (feels like a really hard plastic) on the belt and buttons are a nice antiquated brassy color. The buttons are purely decorative and not at all functional. I got an XL based on my measurements and it was actually a little roomy, but tightening the belt fixed the issue. The belt loop has elastic in the back so it does have some stretch to it. I would definitely recommend knowing your waist/hip/leg measurements to make sure you get the right waist size, but also because this is a fairly long skirt, especially if you're short. The color is accurate to the listing (but looks lighter in my pictures). I bought this for a specific event but like it enough to keep it in my regular routine. I would say it's worth the price and would probably recommend to others.

Closet Must Have ! I ordered a medium. I am 5’8” 120lbs. Honestly these pants far exceeded my expectations! The fit was perfect like a glove, hugged in all the right places. Not too long!! Love these- super comfy material First off I’m on the shorter side (5 foot 3) and weigh 120 pounds. Adorable COMFY baggy jeans I’m 60, 5’5” and 165lbs. I carry excess weight in my tummy which is 36 and my hips are 44.” The size guide did not make much sense. It stated an XL waist measured 23.6 which to me is more like an XXS. Fortunately I’ve purchased jeans and pants from this brand, LifeShe, so I ordered my “usual” size XL in Blue. The fit is slightly loose, which is the style/fit I prefer. These do not fit me low rise, but I’m very short waisted. The waistband is approx 36” relaxed. It’s elasticized but only on the sides and I’m especially happy there are belt loops. The care tag states cotton and polyester, no percentages but it looks like all cotton and it definitely feels like all cotton. It’s lightweight and breathable but not thin or flimsy; a nice quality denim constructed very well. I can’t think of anything I dislike about these jeans - from the deep pockets (the front right has a hidden change pocket) to the contrasting stitching. I love barrel jeans/pants and have quite a few, but I’d consider these more of a reg baggy style, although they do flare out in the hips (these measure 26” laying flat side to side). The length/inseam may not be “super tall friendly.” In my image I’m wearing platform Converses that are about 1.5.” I wish these were just an inch longer in case they shrink. If they do will update my review. I received these through Amazon Vine at a discounted price in exchange for my review. However, I feel at just under $40 the price is very fair. They’re actually 10% off at the time of this review (plus free Prime shipping). I love these so much I purchased the black & white ones.

Initial Impressions I'm 5'2" (157.28cm) and 158~ lbs (71.5kg) Ordered the black pair in the size 8, tag on jean says 8/29. Purchased these to wear to work, 10hr shift in fab & assembly. First Impressions: Stretch is good, thicker fabric kinda scratchy Legs actually look longer with the bootcut, does the curves justice. Solid shade of black. No back gap, might need belt. Fit : 4/5 Fit my legs and especially my thighs and butt (the biggest part of my body) comfortably but is a little looser one my waist/stomach. I could go down a full 2 inches on the waistline but nothing a belt won't help with. The waistline sits right at my bellybutton (35" (89cm) to the floor) and doesn't ride too far down when I bend or squat, I have full coverage. I don't know if going down a size would be the right move for me just because I want them to be comfortable while I'm working and if they're too snug will hamper my range of movement. Length : 4/5 They are a little long, would go with a petite size if possible, but they will be worn with boots so it's not an issue. If you don't mind the hems dragging a bit or some hemming work like me then you'll be fine. Work pants are ment to get dirty. Fabric : 3/5 Seems like a sturdy denim. A bit scratchy but that'll be fixed with an initial wash. They do have a little stretch so if you are in between sizes then you're safe to go on the lower end. Seams are solid and the fabric is thicker than regular fashion jeans. Haven't put it through a workday yet but they seem like they'll hold up. Pockets : 2/5 Better than most jeans but could be bigger. When it comes to pockets size matters. At 16cm deep, just barely over 6", there's room for improvement where there isn't for my phone, keys, wallet, chapstick, and work fob. Can't carry a purse around the warehouse, ya feel? Price : 1.5/5 I personally don't like spending more than $20-$25 on a pair of pants but I need something sturdy and that fits over my boots. I bought these on a 20% off sale for $40 instead of $50 which is the only reason I considered these. Honestly just feels like I'm paying for the brand and not the pants at this point. I did order a pair of Wranglers that were on sale for $30 (29% off $42) that'll be here in a day or two. Depending on how well that pair fits I may return the Dickies and get another pair of W's (or 2, for that price). All that being said, I have not actually worn these to work so if I decide to keep them and they hold up for a long while then maybe they're worth it. For a $50 pair of jeans they at least better last me 3-5 years.

Comfortable and forgiving fit I've never worn Dickies brand jeans, and thus I ordered the same size I would wear in another brand, hoping the fit would be right. Fortunately for me, the sizing was spot-on.The 'Perfect Shape' line has some give (stretch), but not a lot. The pair I received in size 10 fits a bit tight through my thighs and a little loose through my seat, but to be honest - I have a flat rear and my legs are muscular so that probably adds to the issue. I am 5'5" and the length is perfect for boots. At 170 pounds (darn it...) I carry most of my weight in my middle and have a 34" waist and 40" hips.The denim is mid-weight and I feel like it will soften even more with repeated washings and wear. The color is as pictured and looks great right out of the box. Two front pockets, one coin pocket (right side) and two back pockets are as I like it, with a zipper in the front.These jeans are for women who have curves. The fit is flattering and forgiving. They seem well made and I would consider buying more.

Basic Bootcut Jean With Imperfect Fit I like how the Dickies look on my wife and can't detect any obvious issues, but my wife feels these jeans are oddly sized. When I looked more closely with a discernible eye, I concur that it's an imperfect fit. The basic design suggests form fitting comfort and in some areas of the body, it delivers on that point. But where some areas fit right, other spots are incongruous and are not a good match on my curvy wife. First off, the size 10 R is long. My 5'5 ½" wife wore 2 inch heeled boots (see photos) and the leg hem is to the floor. In all fairness, my wife wears size 8-10 pants and perhaps the 8 would work better. These definitely run large. She needs to keep pulling them up as they slide down, even over her curvy hips. She will need to wear a belt and high heels to make them work. They look good around the legs and derriere, but are too wide on the waist and feel loose and bunchy around the hips and crotch. The actual material feels like value fabric. These are not super smooth jeans. They feel and look a wee vintage and in that regard seem like old fashioned jeans of the 80s but with a little more stretch than pure cotton pants of the past. The stitching and weave is decent and the button and zipper work great. They appear to be sturdy, breathable pants that will last a long time. The pockets are nice and deep and there is a slight stretch throughout, but almost too much stretch to the point of slippage and instability. My wife isn't sure if she will wear the Dickies much as she has other shape jeans that look and feel better. But all things considered, it's a good value for a basic classic bootcut jean for the right body type. Just bear in mind that they run long and large. ~ Kort

This fit at least two sizes smaller than the measurements would lead one to expect so I had to order the largest size 4-5X rather than my usual 2X. The larger size fits nicely and the coat is very well made with a good quality stout denim but the sleeves are too long. I’m 5’4” tall and have longer arms than the average woman does but the upper edge of the cuff extends to my fingertips, so the cuff itself is entirely past the tips of my fingers. I can roll them up but I will have to get them shortened because the denim is fairly thick and the rolled up part is too thick and heavy. Overall it’s a really nice coat and I like it very much but the sizing is a problem and the sleeves would be too long even if I was much taller than I am.

Great jean short for the price! I've had a reallyyyyyy hard time finding decent maternity clothing in general, and I'm pretty picky about my jean shorts. I'm the typical American Eagle jean short wearer, so I was hoping these would be stretchy like those, and they sure are!! My ONLY complaint is they don't fit close enough to the bum around the legs (I hate the diaper butt look), but I could've sized down and that would probably be fixed. They are very comfortable and have ample amount of stretch. The length is great too — short enough to be "hip" but not too short to be hoochie mama : ) For reference I'm 5'3, 145lbs, 6 months pregnant, and wore a size small before getting pregnant in all bottoms (was 130lbs). I ordered these in a medium but probably could've ordered a small to fix the fit in the rear. They should consider making a "distressed" version of these!!

Uncomfortable Seem I'm 33 weeks along, 238 pounds, and stand at 5'6. All around these shorts felt nice in quality. The belly has a more loose than spandex material so I'm not sure how long that will hold up around the belly. But, the shorts are nice and snug though verging too tight. And I'm not sure yet if it's because of the size or the fact that the line in the picture I circled literally cuts into your skin. Out of all the stretch these shorts offer, that seem feels like a size too small with absolutely no stretch and it's these shorts biggest downfall. Its an unavoidable issue. Because while you may have had wiggle room to grow, that seem will stop you from fitting in them. I read in another review about it and still thought it was worth the shot considering maternity shorts, in my size, are hard to find. And while I'm almost happy with these. I dont know how long I'll be able to wear them if I gain much more, all because of that seem.

I’m very happy with these shorts! I bought both the light and dark colours. The dark is more blended than it looks in the pictures it’s not such a harshly obvious wave. They are a really perfect length not overly short but not frumpy long either. The Jean material is comfy and stretchy sort of like a jegging but with more structure than cheap jegging shorts so they look like normal Jean shorts. The panel that goes over your belly is made out of a fairly thin material compared to other maternity pants so they seem well suited to summer weather. Not so thin that it seems like it’ll rip but you can see light through the material when you hold it up to a window so I think it’ll be pretty breathable on a hotter day. The only thing to note is that the top of the panel goes up a little higher than my other maternity pants so it might not work for a very short woman but I’m 5”6 with a short torso and the top of it hits my bra but the material is thin enough that it’s not annoying

OK so I am 5' 11" and about 260lbs..measurements 46' 41' 55 hips..and I have been wanting a Vintage denim jacket that actually fits good since forever.. I looked at this for like a week..it's been in the cart but kept ignoring cause the 'XXL sizing was worrying me because a XXL and a 2xl are different sizes a lot of people don't realize.. so I was skeptical..omg it arrived way before it's arrival time of 10 pm next day..got it today and opened the bag.. when I pulled this jacket out.. it smelled vintage lol.. like old denim..that's a plus cause then I know it's not chambray it's real jeans material with no stretch.. and then I opened up the jacket and it was big big.. Then i gently closed my eyes while standing in front the mirror then put it on.. my big long arms slid in with so much ease.. and then the jacket actually met in front a d can be buttoned to my 😮 and surprise.. which is amazing for me. I am never this lucky. I live in nyc and we go through like 6 seasons in one fall day so some jackets are too hot. Plus I have a bunch of patches that's begging for a new home on a piece of clothing..the price at $43 bucks was on point and actually the best 43 bucks I spent in a while. I..waiting on my new pair of Doc Martens on Saturday and I am fall ready. THANK YOU TO THIS COMPANY. THIS JACKET IS PERFECT. A GOOD WASH WITH SOME SOFTENER WILL RID THE NOT SO TERRIBLE SMELL. ..edit...AND IT HAS THE TWO INSIDE POCKETS LIKE OLD JEANS JACKETS DO. 🥰

Close, No Cigar/Odd Construction Okay so my view of these and how I feel about them. Pairs bought:size 14 black, size 15 black.Material Contents: 98%cotton 2%Spandex each pair. I'm 5'6" 185-190lb on my heavy side rn, w/meas. of 38",33",46". Size 13-14 is my usual size currently. These in sz.14 are what I have on in all 4 pics.The waist fits me alright, the shorts as a whole feel like they suck me up with moderate belly/flab control, and is slightly forgiving with a small gap in the back as pictured. Although they're 98%cotton there is almost no breathability and very little stretch in the fabric and waist. The crotch is on the tighter side for me even though they have a longer 7" inseam, the cut is weird through the groin area and I wouldn't want to wear these in a hot humidity environment. Similar to how others have stated, I can see how these won't hold their shape after wear, they wrinkle easily and would have to be ironed even after the dryer, they flare/bow out at the bottom sides, and they are soft in the inside and outside however any kind of marks show up on this type of material especially since it's a darker shade of black. The front pockets are of decent sizing, can be difficult ti access with the shorts tighter around the hip and waist, and the back pockets exist except they're sewn in of which can be carefully cut out and removed. They are naturally snug around my problem area where my belly pooch is however this isn't the case with my other Lee shorts sz14. These are a nice casual short that I'd wear to a business-casual event outside or preferably inside where extrenuous movement wouldn't be involved although these would be my last choice if nothing else was available--this type of short is either for a unique body type, someone who doesn't mind the flaws, or the flaws work for them. Returning and on the hunt again.Happy Shopping y'all 😀

Belt stretches permanently with wear This is a great belt! I feel like I finally found my fashion groove with this belt. It is easy to close but will not come undone. And the design adds that little extra to outfits. I am so happy to have found this belt. I ordered the coffee & brown two-belt package because I was worried the colors would be off in the photos and I wasn't sure which brown would look best with the dress I planned to wear it with. First, let me say that the colors in the pictures are pretty accurate. "Coffee" is a dark brown and "brown" is more of a light brown/dark tan color. After reading the extremely varied reviews about the fit of the belts, I was very nervous to choose. I am 5'6" and weigh about 205 lbs., with most of my weight in my stomach. I ordered the XL (and worried they would be too small). The belt fit around my waist with very little stretch the first time I wore it, and I thought I might order the next size down for the next colors I wanted. But after wearing it 2-3 times, I realized that it didn't even have to stretch to reach around my waist. The belt had stretched permanently after just a couple times wearing it. (See picture -- I have not worn the lighter brown belt yet.) This is a stretchy belt, so it is to be expected that this will happen. I'm not really complaining; I just wanted to make buyers aware of this. I would buy a size down than what you expect will fit you. Not just because it will likely fit you anyway, but because the belts will stretch with wear. Hope this helps. Good luck!

These look better on the model! They have potential though... I think this cargo pocket style has potential, but they look better on the online model than they do on me. For reference, I am 5'7" tall, 130#, and middle-aged! First, the material of these denim shorts is good quality. It is thick and stretchy. They seem like they will be durable shorts for lots of wearing and washing. I would not call them "high-waisted" necessarily. They are not low-rise, but I would have expected the waist to be higher to be considered "high-waisted." My waist is not nearly as toned as the model's, so I have some bulge at the waist in these shorts. If they truly were high-waisted, they could cover my problem area ;). I sized UP to a Medium since they were advertised as stretchy, slim-fitting, and it looked like the models were all wearing them sort of tight. I guess I'm glad I did size up, as that loosened the waist a little, but it made the bottom of the shorts' legs pretty wide/flared like bells. I don't like the "bell" look of the legs, and would rather the legs be straighter. But if you don't want the waist to be tight, I'd recommend you size up as well. Because the waist is small compared to the rest of the shorts. I also wish there were 2 basic, flat, normal jeans pockets on the back, because I feel my butt looks bad with the no-pocket style. The length is a plus, IMO. Many denim shorts are either super short or too long, and I think the length of these (maybe approx 2.5" inseam) is perfect. Thank you!

It works! I am 5’5 and around 165 lbs. I purchased this because I love jumpsuits but didn’t expect much. Well, I love the way it looks! Unfortunately, I sized up as per the reviews saying that it had no give and needed a size bigger if I had curves. It was not the case for me. The waist elastic was a bit loose and there was some gaping around the hips as you can see in the picture, so I sent it back and purchased a size M (pictures are of a size L). The fabric is really nice, thicker denim and looks like it will stretch out with time so I think the smaller size will be more flattering. It had a bit of a scent on arrival so you will need to wash it before wearing it. The pants touched the top of my feet (a bit longer than ankle length). It has buttons and not snaps. The top is a bit bigger than the bottom (another reason why I decided to go down a size). But the construction is very nice. If you like jumpsuits and the 90s look, this is for you! Update: I ordered the M Size and it is a bit tight. Such a bummer! Since I’m between sizes, I’m keeping the smaller one and hoping it will feel better once the extra Christmas weight goes away 🫣

Nice, comfortable, sized down one and fit was perfect I was looking for a mid to high wasted pair of jeans that are on the thinner/looser side and can be worn throughout the year, even in the warmer season. There is nothing super special about the way they look, but they can be dressed up or down and are extremely comfortable. I am usually a small or size 26 in jeans (5'4" 118 lbs) but had read these run large so I got the extra small and they fit perfectly. These are good everyday jeans and I'd recommend them.

Good quality, fits well... I ordered the Lookbook Denim Jeans for Women in Pecan Brown Size XXL. To start, these are not high waisted pants. They are hip huggers, meaning they sit low on your waist and will not restrict your tummy if you have a few extra pounds. Furthermore, they state they jeans are stretch. They have a slight stretch to them, but they are not what is normally considered a stretch pant. Finally, these are not baggy on the average girl. They are boot cut/flare leg, but not baggy.With all of that said, I am 5 foot, 5 inches tall, 275 pounds, wear an 18/20 USA size and a 2x/3x top. My waist measurement at belly button level is 46 inches. My upper thighs and calves are thicker than most. I was concerned these pants would not fit, however, the Lookbook Denim Jeans for Women in Pecan Brown Size XXL fit comfortably. I will say there is a bit of tightness in the knee area, but I tend to have this issue due to my thick legs. I am not use to a hip hugger pant, so was genuinely surprised that these pants fit tight, but comfortably, as they sit under my "jelly roll", so there is none of that constrictive feeling on your stomach. I also like my pants longer and I found these to fit perfectly, touching the ground, giving them room to shrink a bit.Overall, the Lookbook Denim Jeans for Women in Pecan Brown in Size XXL are well made, the denim is thicker than most and I absolutely love the color. The fit is surprisingly good and the hip hugger style makes them extremely comfortable. So if you are a curvy girl like me, check these out. Five stars.

Love the jeans...not well put together EDIT - I received a second pair of these jeans today after the last pair having a crazy amount of loose strings I went ahead and tried one more time. I am extremely happy that I did, the second pair are totally free of loose strings aside from two by the zipper that can be trimmed with no bad effect to the pants. I tried them on so the sizes definitely seem to be regulated as the last pair and this one both fit me basically the same way. They are stretchy and soft and feel like a sturdy enough pair of jeans they seem like they will last a good amount of time. I am happier with this purchase now and I have upped from 3 to 4 stars.I got these in today and immediately tried to put them on! Tried...I had to trim a string that is sewn in on one side of one of the flared bottom, and also sewn into another side, so it crossed the opening of the pants and caught my toes painfully before I trimmed it. I turned the pants inside out and there are quite a few strings, one bundle of which looks like it actually compromises the integrity of the cloth, which just means they may fall apart rather quickly. I am 5'6" 160ish pounds and the size 10 fit me well, I just wish there wasn't an issue on the inside of the pants.

Cute distressed shorts My teen daughter weighs 115 lbs and she’s almost 5’6” tall. She wears shorts in size small or 4. I ordered these shorts in color 21148 size small for her and they are a perfect fit. The fabric content is 85% cotton 15% polyester. The shorts look exactly as they do in the pictures in the listing. There’s a lot of distressing and rips on the front. Underneath most of those rips there are white interior pockets that show through the rips where the shorts are being worn. On the back the rips are only on the back pockets. As you can see on my picture there was some uneven fringe distressing done on the back of the legs openings. My daughter trimmed the too long strings with scissors after taking the picture. The shorts are more skin revealing on the front but they have a full coverage on the back. They are high waisted. The stitching is ok overall but there was one spot in the front with missing stitch, and a few others will loose threads. I’m taking a star off for those sewing imperfections but my daughter really likes the fit and the look of these denim shorts and she wears them often.

Really Great Shorts! I got these blue “high waisted” shorts during a flash sale for like $18 and I’m SOO glad I did! They fit wonderfully! Really Great Shorts! I got these blue “high waisted” shorts during a flash sale for like $18 and I’m SOO glad I did! They fit wonderfully! Fantastic for what they are OK so first things first, I feel like some reviews need a but of a reminder that these are inexpensive Amazon shorts. They arent BAD quality, just don't be expecting the nicest of zippers or buttons. I have huge thighs compared to by waist (I wish that looked as good as it sounds but I promise it does not). My thighs will pull up any shorts at all unless I go full out knee length which I am not doing. Finding shorts to fit my thighs is near impossible. This will be my third pair. The stretch and little side slit helps immensely. For size, I got a medium. I'm about 175 5'6" 28" waist 43" hips. I can't see the size chart right now but I think that put me at an xl. After reading the reviews I kept seeing "i sized down and would have been fine doing so again". And if you have big thighs you know you have to err on the side of a little tight or you get that excess bulge of fabric in the middle that's not a good look. These fit super comfortably. As you can see, more on the snug side but not tight. They have plenty of give. If you don't have the spare fabric when you sit issue you can size down just one I think. Kinda wish there were options without lots of fringe that didn't involve cuffs (those REALLY don't work with thighs), because I'm not 13 anymore, but honestly I'm gonna trim it off, replace the buttons, and these will be worn a lot. Overall 10/10, I'm happy.

Stylish/Perfect/Comfortable!! These shorts are perfect!! I bought them to wear over my swimsuit, and they are so so so cute and stylish, but also modest... so I dont have half my buttcheeks hanging out. As a mommy of two very young kiddos, I hold my dress standards to a higher level, and no longer feel comfortable showing too much skin (totally my personal preference, so if you like your butt hanging out of your shorts.. then you go girl.. I just dont). So I really love these shorts for this reason, but on top of that.. they're also sooo dang cute and incredibly comfortable. I will say, as far as how they fit... that they are really big on me.. like I can pull them out and have over 6 inches of gap between my lower back and the back of the shorts. But I honestly dont care.. because they still feel great on and look cute. I think they're actually true to size, and I dont believe they run big at all... I just run small. Haha I'm only 5'3" and barely 100lbs... and I have to shop in the kids' section, so I usually expect adult clothing to be much too big for me. With that being said, these shorts are really amazing. You get the cute look of a cut off, without your entire butt hanging out. They're the perfect length, and I highly recommend them!!

The only down side is the color. I ordered Black but they are more like off black/dark grey. I will buy another pair in dark denim color.

The stretch is REAL! I'm so mixed in my opinion of these shorts. I am 5'4 and just under 150 lbs. I usually float between Medium and Large, so I went with a Large because I wanted the relaxed fit at thighs - which was perfect!! But unfortunately, they were overall too big. The fabric is very soft and stretchy. If you're in between sizes like I am, maybe try the lower size. The cut-off hem is flattering and the length is just long enough that I think I could have went with the Medium.

If you are going for daisy duke these are the ones Let me start by saying I’m a plus sized girl with all my weight on the bottom half, so shorts are difficult. I’m going to keep them, more as a swimsuit coverup than an everyday wear pair of shorts. They are fine for being out on the boat but there’s no way I would wear them anywhere else.

My favorite jeans! I love these retro hippy jeans! I had to send a few pairs back, but now I have a size that fits just right. For reference I’m 5’7” 150lbs with a thick middle and thin legs. I’m 59 y/o but tend to shop in the juniors department because of my legs being thin. I’m a woman’s size 10 so that’s what I ordered at first. These jeans run large in the middle and they’re very stretchy. I sent them back and got a size 8. They were also a bit too roomy in the middle. They fit snug, but I could stretch the waist out several inches. Pushing my luck, I sent them back and got a size 6. This girl hasn’t been a size 6 since the 70’s! They fit and felt great. The waist is not too tight to expose a muffin top and actually still has a bit of room if I stretch it out. Posting photos to show measurements. If you have thicker legs, there is still plenty of stretch in these pants as long as you like them snug. The length was perfect even after I machine washed them in cold water. I hung to dry overnight then put in the dryer for 5 minutes just to soften up. Directions say hand wash, but I raised five kids so I never had time for such nonsense :)

Flattering comfort for full figured Goddesses! These prefaded stretchy jeans look like the advertised photos. The flared embroidered bell bottom is very flattering for full figures and the stretch makes them super comfortable. Im 5’4” and weigh 175. I regularly wear size 14-16 but got size 12 as they have alot of stretch in them. If i wanted them a bit looser id suggest the 14. I have bought three pairs from this company and they all are made and fit the same. So order one size down from your regular good fit. One thing to note as you can see on the model these jeans are on the shorter side maybe my fullness pulls the length up but still its amazing since most jeans are made for tall thin people. Awesome pants, not too heavy Great pants! The stars are subtle but so cool and my daughter loves them! She's 11 years old, 67" (5'7", 170 cm) tall, weighs about 134 lbs (61kg, 9.5 stone). Yes, she's really that tall lol. My ONLY problem with the pants was that the buttonhole wasn't cut. It was sewn, but there wasn't a cut for it. I just grabbed my pocketknife an made a cut inside the buttonhole stitching, and the button went right thru, no problem. The elastic waist in the back will ensure these will fit her for quite a while. I did buy the 3x based on the measurements, and they're probably a tiny bit loose, but not enough to fall down.

Good not amazing thi I like these jeans, I like the color and wash a lot they’re same as the picture and I am keeping them but they have a few things I don’t love. I knew they were going to be more stiff because of the style of jean so that’s a given but they’re not unflattering. I’m not saying these are going to be my favorite jeans that compliment my body super well but I needed a lighter wash jean with no rips. Btw I am 5’2” and fluctuate between 125-130lbs with a more athletic build (thicker thighs and butt) and I originally bought the size 28 after reading reviews when I’m normally a 27. 28 was too big on my waist and gave me a weird V shape in the front, wasn’t a camel toe but in my third pic you can see what I mean. So I re-ordered them in a 27 and that’s what I’m wearing in the pics. I wish they came in 27 short or clarified what 27 vs 27 regular means. Anyways these aren’t 5 star jeans but are the same as pictured so that’s nice. Just be aware that there’s almost no stretch but I assume they’ll break down after wearing and washing them a few times.

My favorite jeans in college resurrected These are more or less the exact jeans I loved and wore to death in the early 1990s. I’m almost 5’2”, 105#, not curvy, and the 24 fits perfectly. I can get into 23 as well but wanted more room. I don’t need a belt with these. The pair I received are exactly the right length, just below the ankle, but I know QC varies so I think I got lucky. They are super comfy and have nice deep usable pockets. I also appreciate that I don’t have to worry about showing my whole butt to anybody when I bend over. I’m not gonna say they’re more flattering than low- or mid-rise jeans, because they’re not, but I don’t feel like I have to take them off the second I get home and there is something to be said for that.

Bad quality. Grommets only connected to one side. I bought this belt not only for the look, but also because grommets can add extra support to the belt. The grommets don't have a back connection so they're not really grommets in the traditional sense. They pop out easily with the slightest pressure because there is nothing supporting them on the back side. They should not be relied on for actually holding pants up. The photos (and probably the positive reviews) show people who are basically just wearing this for show. If that's what you're looking for, they are certainly cheap, so one start for that. The other is that despite no grommets on the sizes I needed, they held up my pants for a year, so one star for that too. But you can see how much damage happened over one year. I'm relatively slim man (6' and between 32' and 34' waist) and I don't wear heavy jeans or work in a physically demanding job. Buyer beware!

Awesome Flattering Short Shorts. I always thought this design kinda looked like a waitress’s apron. But, luckily, in person these shorts are actually pretty amazing. Good thing they’re so stretchy too, or I probably wouldn’t have fit in them. I’m 5’7”, 115lbs, 27”Waist, 34”Hips, and got an XS because short legs and hips are always super wide on me. Should’ve gotten the Small. The waistband is very tight, causing spillage when I hardly have any meat on my bones anyway, and the skirt panel pulls at the button. BUT, I still think they look incredible -I just won’t wear short tops. The front skirt is so uniquely stylish, flattering, and balances out well with the super short and tight back. I love all the pockets and, overall, the shorts seem very well-made. Also, they’re surprisingly comfortable. This is a great choice of shorts with fashionable flair. They will look fabulous on you. Just, go with your true size.

Happy and disappointed I weigh 125 and I’m 5ft 3 inches. I originally ordered a medium and that was too big for me so I ordered a small. Today I wore my first pair and the small fit like a glove. Not too long with heel boots. I loved them all day. Until I spilled something on it and I spot cleaned it. The spot cleaned up fast. I took them off and noticed a circle from where the water had expanded during the cleaning using soap and water ONLY. I threw into the wash and when it was done the circle is still there. Clearly you cannot spot clean these. I’m so disappointed to have wasted my money on a item that is now not available to wear again with a huge circle created from a simple spot clean. Warning to wash before first wear would have been nice. Or you risk the dye going away with spot clean or rain. I’m so upset and nervous to use the two other pairs I bought also.

Cute but they wear terribly I needed a last minute outfit for a themed party and thought these would be perfect. I am “midsize” - size 12-14 and I’m 5’3. I did have to hem them by 2 inches, but otherwise they fit perfectly until I started moving and dancing as the night went on. It started as weird pooching/looseness in the fabric on the tummy and upper thighs, then progressively stretched to the point of having to keep holding my pants up so they wouldn’t slide down while I did anything! There are no belt loops… which is stupid, and there is no elasticity, so the pants will stretch but won’t revert until washing. Not worth the money in my opinion.. I would have returned had I not hemmed them. Perhaps I will add some belt loops with the extra fabric or regift.

I am 5’3 and weigh about 128-135 depending on the season😉 I ordered size M and was a little hesitant because I needed a longer inseam to allow for 2” heels. These did not disappoint! In flat feet they hit the floor, and the length is perfect for my 2-2.5” heels.

I hate Jeans...but these could be an exception Alright so I grew up always wearing pants, but then I gained weight and jeans are my worst enemy. Ask any of my friends and you will learn in always in my skirt and I admire retro fashion. So since I'm loosing weight and need new Jean's I figured why not try something new? I found these and the reviews looked good. Some of them said to order one down so I measured myself and then chose my size and ordered. Well it came 20 minutes ago and here are the pros and cons. Pros: -they feel great -they look great -the back pocket is big and not fake Cons: -there is no front pocket(its not even there and fake) -i didnt read/expect the waist to be elastic but that could be my fault. Because they do stretch a little they tend to fall down when I move so I need to get a belt.(witch is a con in itself for me) More notes: I'm about 5'5" and the bottom doesn't have a lot of fabric on the floor just a little bit about 2 inches.

I wore them from 7am to 6pm and they looked exactly like they did when I put them on that morning. They are stretchy but they do NOT bag out at all. The quality of these pants is excellent. I’m actually ordering another color right now. I think the dark grey and also the charcoal color. I highly recommend them!

Very comfortable. I got 4 pairs. I am 5’4”, 140lbs and I got this in a size medium. My legs are about 34 inches long. My waist is about 26 or 27 inches depending on how much I’ve eaten and my hips are about 40 or 41 inches at the moment. I usually struggle to buy jeans, but this pair is very comfortable and hugs. My curves the way it’s supposed to unlike most jeans and it doesn’t have that weird gaping space in the back. I could wear these with or without a belt. They’re comfortable and stretchy. I’m posing funny in 2 pictures because I wanted to show how much they easily stretch. I got them in four different colors, but I don’t recommend the white pair if you’re a perfectionist. The white pair collects too much fat is and hair to it. It happened to me at work with a scarf. That’s why I gave it 4 stars. If you go by the size chart, then it is true to size and like I said it stretches so it has gift to it. Not trying to be rude but whoever said they run small was in denial about their measurements. They’re as comfortable as my cotton jeggings from Uniqlo. I recommend these jeans especially if you’re curvy like me.

Beautiful Gorgeous Stunning This belt is absolutely stunning in person. It almost feels like cheating the system to get it for as inexpensive as it is. The belt is made out of a silver glitter faux leather that is thick and durable and the glitter does not flake off. Each of the rhinestones is riveted into the pleather and is super secure, they aren't going to be falling off. It has heavy duty hardware encrusted with rhinestones, the belt is just heavy weighted and feels super expensive. The belt runs true to size, I have a 38 in waist and ordered the L that fits 39-44in and have plenty of room to adjust if I wear low rise pants or otherwise. I've got the silver belt that has the iridescent rhinestones through the middle, I love the little pop of rainbow that they give. Although the belt says it's western style I actually find that it works well with many styles of clothing and think that this belt is an absolutely beautiful accent to any outfit to make it super fancy.

They will shrink. I ordered three pair in prints and was not disappointed. The colors are vibrant and the patterns are beautiful.(5'7" / 160 /curvy). Ordered one size up, so XL would have been too small. The 2XL feels good. 4 stars not 5 because they did shrink and also Ithe fit on the waist, the elastic sits low on the hips if I want a full length. I tried them on before washing and they fit perfectly but I washed them and hung to dry and then 10 min on Low in dryer. They came out about 2" short on the legs and the overall fit more snug and is similar to sweat pants. The fabric is comfortable and not thin, but light on the body and translucent in the light. I'm happy and considering buying more.

100% cotton, dry clean recommended The size XL fit my 180 lb, 5'3" body. BUT girls with thick thighs like me won't get that baggy pant look you see on the models. That said the pants are nice. Elastic leg bands fall just above the ankle and the button detail is a nice touch. I didn't realize that detail faces forward, not to the side like I thought, but it's fine. Also from the pictures, I thought the pockets were wider/bigger which isn't a problem but just pointing it out. Fabric has a chemical smell so you need to wash before wearing. I'm holding my breath they don't fall apart in washer since professional dry cleaning is recommended for these 100% cotton pants, but the tag also says wash separately. I have to learn to read descriptions more carefully, but when I saw them I just had to have them, especially for only $19!! Ordered another pair in XXL to see if I get that baggy leg look.

I wanted a pair of crop jeans for spring and summer. I’m tired of finding crop pants in regular length that I would have to shorten them. These were 6P and the length is perfect, about 4” above my ankles and just below the wide part of my calf. Mid calf is not flattering on my since mine are muscular not slender. The fabric has some stretch as well as the waistband. They are comfortable and flattering. I first got them inn 4P but they were a little snug so I exchanged them for the 6P. My others in 4P were fine size wise. I’m 5’3” and 125 lbs. the smaller size would have stretched out eventually, but I wanted them right away. Note: the fabric has some slight imperfections placed purposefully near top front of thighs for the distressed look.

Order your normal size I live for leopard print! The first time I ordered this jacket Amazon had suggested that the large would fit me best, so I ordered up from my normal size medium. I assumed that maybe this piece ran small. Nope…it was WAY too big for me, and not in a cute, oversized way. So I returned it and ordered a medium. Still oversized, but it actually looked adorable. I think I definitely could’ve sized down and still had plenty of room to move around in. It’s meant to run a little large though, so if you’re looking for a snug fit then order a size (or two) down. Otherwise go with your regular size and you’re golden. One thing I noticed is that the print is clear and defined. Sometimes the images/prints on clothing are a bit blurry, but this one looks pretty pristine. The price point isn’t too hefty…especially for denim, and the material isn’t itchy or incredibly stiff. I wouldn’t suggest wearing it in cooler weather unless you really plan on layering up. I love that it can be styled in so many different ways…with jeans, leggings, skirts, trousers, tees, sweaters, dresses, button-ups, etc. I feel like the possibilities are endless. For reference I’m 5’3”, 152lbs, 36C and ordered a medium. The only reason for the 4 stars rather than 5 is because the first medium I received had a broken button.

Meat & Taters. XL. Size 16 woman. 5'5. 168 lbs. Super stretchy. Low waist, so keep in mind they will pull down slightly in the rear when sitting, if you have a full peach bottom. Embroidery is nice and intact. Denim is a medium weight and seems durable. Length is perfect. Not short and not overly long foe being 5'5" tall. They feel comfy. They do not suck your tummy in, though. There is a very slight flair at the bottom of each leg, not overly exaggerated. Update: I've had these jeans a few weeks now and I love them! They wash up nice, haven't lost their stretch, do not bag out in the butt, and haven't shrank after washing. These are my new favorite jeans. The denim is a decent thickness. They should last several years. They are a very comfy pair of jeans.

Wrong measurements I got these for Christmas from a family member and I swear I thought she had sized them down when ordering. But no it is just lying about the measurements. I actually have both pairs of jeans now and the flower embroidered one is great they are very comfortable, stretchy and I’ve had them for about 5 months and they are still not even worn out a little. The star ones however- in the same size might I add- are terrible. Not stretchy or comfortable, in fact, after spending 5 minutes trying to get them on, I measured them and the waist was the only slightly correct size and I’m really dissatisfied and upset. I got medium and it says the waist is supposed to be about 39 inches but when I measured it it was 32 at most I honestly don’t remember how much the out-seam was but I measured 38. I admit I’m a little curvy at 5’4”, 150lbs but this can’t be right. I’m upset because I can’t even return them since they were a gift and I don’t have the receipt.

Size chart in listing was a great help! Whoa! This is a NICE belt!! It's got an easy to see detailed design, and the embossed design on the buckle is stunning! Also, this is one of those soft belts that conforms well to the body and does not dig into my hip bone. I wore this belt all day on the day I received it and it stayed comfortable throughout the day. Also very happy that it would be easy to change out the buckle if I wanted, just 2 snaps and your can remove the buckle to replace with another.For reference, I am 5'6" and weigh 111 lbs. I usually wear somewhere between 4-6 depending on the pant, so following the size chart in this listing and ordering the 31-33 gave a great fit. There are a couple more notches if I ever need them so there is some leeway on the fit. As of now, this is without question the most comfortable belt I own. I have no hesitation in recommending this 5-star belt!

Happy with these overall but here are some things I would have wanted to know before ordering: The drawstring is not functional. The ties are just for show. The pink is more of a dusty rose. They are already oversized so you don’t need to size up. They wrinkle like crazy with wear so don’t bother ironing them. Give up on having the fabric look smooth like the photo and just settle in to the wrinkled vibe. They are recommended to hang dry so do size up if you’re like me and don’t have the patience to hang dry casual wear. They will mostly shrink up length wise so keep that in mind if you’re tall. They arrived smelling like they were sewn by a cigarette and the smell didn’t completely come out after the first wash. All that being said, they are cute and comfy and would medium recommend them. Probably better options available but if you see these and like the style you’ll probably be happy with your purchase. Size reference: I ordered a large and have a size 24” waist so the elastic waist is forgiving in either direction. I could probably fit another 1.5 of me in here and be comfortable.

These are like leggings not jeans and are stretchy. The dark blue are not blue Updated review.I ordered gray and dark blue. I recieved the dark blue and they are not dark blue at all. They look to me as if these are a purple. A light purple so I contacted amazon bc I really thought that they mistakenly sent the wrong color. So amazon sent me out another pair. I recieved them today and these are exactly the same color as the ones that I still have to return back to them. So just letting people know that they are not dark blue. Would be very cute if they got the dye correct and actually made them dark as shown in the picture. This is not a camera picture color difference. It's a huge difference in color and not even blue a all.. I posted 2 pics of what they say is dark blue. The gray ones are actually gray. Not dark gray but gray. I took 2 stars away bc of this and the annoyance plus having a return them back to UPS for refunds because of their mistake. I like these a lot. They are comfortable and easy to move around in bc they stretch . I've read reviews about broken pockets or rips in them. Mine came in just fine . I have no issues with the ones that I have received. They are not buttlifting at all . I can't remember if they were advertised as being so but they are not. I'm going to be using these as gym pants bc that is what they look and feel like . Oh and all pockets are real usable pockets . I'm 5"5 and these are just at my ankles so not tall girl friendly at all. They are not see thru either. I recommend these as workout / yoga pants or even if you have an operation like I have bc they're not tight around the tummy area .

A bit cheap but kind of cool The fabric of these pants are a little bit cheap, but they’re definitely thicker than I thought they were going to be so that’s nice. And they aren’t see-through which, of course I love. So I’m on the fence about the quality, but the fabric does have this slightly fuzzy texture, I’m not sure how else to put it. But most importantly, I actually think they look pretty good on, and they are true bellbottoms as opposed to being called bellbottoms, and actually being boot cut, lol. They aren’t particularly versatile, because they are a very specific style, but I’m digging it. I think they are in fact pretty cool, and I do like the era and vibe, so I’m actually looking forward to wearing them and seeing what other people think! Lol. I think these are a size medium and I am 5’5” 125 lbs 32-27-34. They are a little tight around my stomach right now but that’s because I’m about 10 pounds heavier than I normally am so I think when I am back in shape soon, these will fit perfectly! I would certainly recommend them if you are also into this late 60s era of clothing because the pattern and the colors are spot on. I hope this review is and you have a fantastic day!

Really thin, long, not really as expected but comfortable I ordered the Womens Sexy High Waisted Flare Pants Boho Printed Stretchy Bell Bottom Leggings. I wasn't sure what to expect when I ordered these, but they are not really as expected. I ordered the size small, but technically I needed an extra small which they did not have. So, going into it I felt they were going to be a little bit big which they absolutely were and almost so much where I won't be able to wear them out as planned with a cute top. However, that is my own fault for ordering a size larger and taking a gamble. They are very soft, but they are incredibly thin. They are not as thin as leggings but definitely not thick at all like they appear in the pictures. Because they are so big on me, I can't really get an idea of what they would look like if they had a slim fit. I'm not even sure if an extra small would give the slim fit, actually, like the photos. I am 5'4, and yes they are long which I could tell, and if I just pull them up really high they are fine. I can also flip the waistband over, but that changes the entire look to be even worse than they were before so I won't do that. I noticed the stripes are not the same as the pictures. Right down the middle and towards the Lady area, there are white stripes, and it actually gives the illusion that there is a triangle shape down there, and it's actually pretty funny and not appealing. If they were dark like the pictures, then that would be something different. Even though I wouldn't wear these out because of all these situations, they are incredibly comfortable and feel good to wear around the house which is what I will use them for so they are not getting wasted, and they are more comfortable than most house pants that I have, so that is a plus, thank you.

Great length for tall(er) ladies This review is for the “ Womens High Waisted Jeans Comforts Straight Leg Loose Stretchy Lightweight Tummy Control Trendy Jeans for Women”. I am tall 5’10” with a long inseam and always have trouble finding pants to fit me. These showed a 42.5” total length and when I saw the photos showing the large bottom seam stitching I got them. They have almost 2.5” extra material in the big bottom seam. I let the seam out and stitched them to the longest possible length. And yay, I got tall jeans! These jeans are high waisted and have wide legs. They are stretchy in the right places and are very comfortable, I washed and dried them and they did very well. I am not a big fan of the “whiskers”, they are more pronounced than in the listing pictures. It does not feel like they any tummy control All in all these are good jeans. The competing market is huge and prices range from $15 to $1500. At $41 they are a decent buy if you want wide leg, high waist and stretch.
