Good fit, comfortable, nice looking jeans Written by my wife: I am 5 ft tall, 125 lbs, 28ish inch waist, 38ish inch hips, and I ordered my usual size 4 in these jeans. They fit my waist, hips, and bottom great. The fabric has just enough stretch to be comfortable, but it is not thin and cheap, it is a nice quality denim. I like the color with just a subtle amount of fading. I also like the thick hem. The length is a bit too long for me, as expected, and the legs are a tiny bit too wide, so my friend is going to alter them for me to make them just right. When measuring the length, she noticed that one leg was slightly longer than the other, so a sewing quality issue I guess. I washed and dried my jeans without any issues.

Right amount of thickness and stretch Fit/Sizing - I am 5'8" tall, U.S. women's size 6 (usually) or 28 jeans, long torso. Checked size chart (garment measurements), my waist/hip measurement is between the size 6 and 8. Ordered size 6 expecting a little stretch. Fits comfortably snug in waist, hip and upper half of my butt, falls fairly straight down from there. My suggestion- order the size that includes your measurements at, or up to the next size. For example, order size 6 if your waist is from 26.8"-28.3". Order size 8 if your waist is 28.4"-30", order size 10 if your waist is 30.1"-30.7", and so on. These are very high waisted even on my lanky torso; when I sit down the waist hikes up above my belly button. I like that just fine, but something to consider if you are more petite. Legs are shorter than I'd prefer which is expected, because I am too tall for regular sizes and too short for special tall sizes. It's super annoying but I'm sure there would be more complaints if they were longer so can't exactly blame the pants. Materials/Design - Maybe the other colors use different denim, but I wouldn't call this thick, scratchy old-school denim. I'd describe it as midweight, slightly stretchy but holds its shape well all day, and relatively smooth. Not crazy about the whiskers, but not a dealbreaker. Considering how many pairs of jeans I have to try on in a store before finding one I like, was pretty surprised at the quality and fit of these. I take time to write honest reviews that I would find , whether favorable or not.

They have potential but a LOT of fabric I ordered these jeans with the intent on having a comfortable, loose fitting basic denim in my closet. These do have a lot going for them. The fabric is stretchy and comfortable. I love the high waist and the wider hemmed bottoms. The sizing seems 'close' but maybe just slightly off. I ordered my regular size of 12 with the expectation that the waist might be a bit too tight based on the measurements given. They actually fit perfectly in the waist so I would suggest they might run slightly large to their own measurement chart. On the downside, I knew that these were wider in the leg but there's almost too much fabric without the kind of shaping to help make it work. At 5'4", they are also a bit too long. I'm not so worried about the length because I can make it work with a chunky loafer or boot but overall, these are not particularly flattering on me and I don't think I'll wear them out much. I'll still give them a chance...maybe after a few more washes/dries, they'll work better for me but for now, I would just call them 'okay'.

These jeans are really nice! The waist is slightly smaller and I think that helps to give the illusion of an hourglass figure. These jeans are really cute and comfortable but they are LONG! I am 5'2" and I had to role the entire bottom up. I think at some point I am just going to cut it to fit my height better, but the quality of these is really nice! They are flexible and great for daily wearing. The one thing I am iffy about is the high waist of these is *really* high, like almost to my chest high. I have a short torso so I am sure that is why. So if you have a short torso, keep that in mind.

The fit is a little snug for a typical size 4, but the stretchiness makes it manageable. Remember to size up for a more comfortable fit. They are slightly shorter than expected, ending at my ankles. If they were half an inch to an inch longer, they would suit my preference perfectly. I may be able to adjust the length by not pulling them up all the way. For reference, my height is 5'7".The jeans have a slight odor, likely due to the dye, which I trust will dissipate in the wash. I love them so much that I didn't wait to wash them first and wore them right away.

Great Leggines Lots of Compression! These are very nice leggings. They are super soft and have that buttery, soft, and silky feeling to them. There is a lot of compression, and took a little work to get them on, but once on I think they looked good and felt great! I'm 5'6, and the length they came at fit great, but it seems they can simply be cut to shorten as needed. The inside has a sort of built-in band to create a lift. It feels a little strange at first but I think i will get use to it. It's not uncomfortable per se, but you "notice" it when wearing them. Overall, I really like these leggings.

Very Comfortable Denim Dress with Pockets! This was a very comfortable denim dress that was not too stiff and not too flimsy. Overall, the quality was better than I expected. The buttons work perfectly, being snug enough to hold the denim together and yet easier enough to button and unbutton. I'm 5'8" and I thought the length was perfect. It wasn't too short or too long. I really liked the deep pockets that could easily hold a phone, keys, or small wallet. The pockets are somewhat hidden on the sides and designed in a manner that does not stand out. A quick look at the dress and you might miss them. The sleeves were long enough to fit my longer arms and the elastic wrists helped prevent them from being too long. Overall, the dress has a baggy feel and is free flowing. In some ways this can be flattering as it hides your stomach. Other times, the extra baggy look can make you look bigger. I'm not sure that I would call it flattering, but it's not bad. Overall, I did like this dress a lot. It strikes a lot of balance and I feel it can be versatile. I do recommend this dress for most anyone looking for a denim dress. I really like the way the collar dresses up the look, too.

I try to buy products made in the USA to support jobs and the manufacturing industry in my country. The listing for this belt clearly says Made in USA (as of 10/18/23). See photo which shows belt was made in Cambodia. I have nothing against Cambodians, but I’m not really fond of deceptive sales practices. For me, this deducts two stars from my review. I really like the belt. The leather is soft and pliable, just what I was looking for. I like the color and the simplicity of design. I wouldn’t have purchased it, had the listing been accurate. I am returning it and looking for a product that will support American manufacturing jobs. Too bad...I really liked the belt.

Fun slouchy shirt Just for reference, I am 5'5", 150 lbs. I saw a shirt on the tv show Shrinking and loved it, came to Amazon and searched for "denim pullover" and in about two minutes found it - it's this shirt. I was so happy to see it came in several colors of denim. I bought the grey and I love it. It's very wide but cropped, it's actually a lot less heavy weight of fabric than it looks, which is good for me. Some pockets would be nice of course, but not a deal breaker! I might get this in another color and do some embroidery on it.

Total Beach Vibes I’m a 5’5” 165lbs woman. I’m also a 40B and per the brand’s size guide provided, I could’ve easily worn the smallest size. However at age 60, I don’t like my tops too short or anywhere near a cropped look, so I ordered the XL and I love the fit. I actually prefer my clothing to fit loose (think Free People) and this top does so very comfortably without looking sloppy. Actually, this is a pretty good dupe for Free People’s Jude Denim Pullover, except the price, thankfully. The FP one retails for $128. This initially caught my eye because it’s a great dupe, but I ordered it mainly for the fiber content. True to product description and the attached care tag, this is 100% cotton. It’s a lighter weight cotton, but not thin - it’s very soft and breathable. We live in coastal Fl and this will be perfect for cooler evening walks on the beach. I love the unfinished bottom hem and the block stitching throughout. I feel the quality and construction of this top is excellent. Although I received this through Amazon Vine at a reduced cost, I would totally pay full price for this. I’d love to see this in a turquoise or even white. I washed this on cold and dried on low. It came out nicely, but some may want to give it a quick steam. I like that it looks a little rough hewn after washing; lends to the whole beachcomber vibe. My husband also mentioned that since it runs large it’ll be great for layering when it’s cold. This brand, PLNOTME, also has a store here on Amazon with some really super cute items.

Really Cute! This is pretty close IRL to the listing. I am 5'8" and about 133, size 4/6, with a 30DD chest. On me, the small size seems a little shorter than it does on the model. The denim also seems a touch stiffer and less flowy. I am wondering if the fabric might soften up after a few washings, but I am not mad at it now. The sleeves have a nice structure, and even the small is nice and roomy. For a really oversized look, order up one size but it absolutely not necessary. If you are very petite it might swallow you up, but I think it is fun!

Feels unfinished and weird cut to it. I am 5'7" and 160lbs with a 31" waist I normally wear between a size M and L, I got this in a size L and it was slightly oversized.This gave me Free People vibes and looks to be a dupe of one of their other shirts but I really wasn't impressed with how it looked on or the quality. The edges are unfinished and did have a small line of stitching to prevent further unraveling, but the unfinished edges didn't look intentional...they just looked sloppy.The fit to this is weird. The arms were really long and there are no buttons or elastic so you can't roll them up and have them stay up. The material has no give to it, so you can't push them up your arms either. The cut was very oversized but strangely short in length and the slits up the sides were oddly placed. I just thought it was a very unflattering and non-functional piece.The material is a think but strangely stiff denim, a stiffness I might expect out of a thicker denim but not out of a demin this thin. It was uncomfortable to wear and unflattering. I won't be keeping and would struggle to recommend this, especially at this higher price point. This looked and felt cheap to me.

Perfect fit, super comfy, stretchy but not too stretchy and exactly as pictured. I'm 5'3" 148 lbs and I ordered a medium. I carry almost all my weight in my in my legs and tend to have an issue with pants (of any type) being pulled down from my waist/butt by my damn legs and calves. These jeans don't give that potential feeling at all. They have not been washed yet as the party is next week so I'll update this review if needed once they've been worn for a length of time. As of right now though, 100% recommend.

Happy with them! I picked this picture to share so others know what to expect. The product perfectly matches the description. They fit perfectly and the color was spot on. Super cute! Excited to have for warmer weather. Being short capri's sometimes still too long but this was a great length for 5'2. 128 pounds I ordered a M.

Stretchy, flexible material. Neutral: I cannot speak to machine washing them, I hand wash. They recommend cold water. However, I do like to dry my pants in a dryer -- and that's not recommended with these. Drip drying these takes a mite bit of time. Yay! They fit and they feel comfortable. I'm built-low-to-the-ground 5'2" and ordered a Medium. They come down to within tolerable "high waters" to my ankles. For those who are taller, they will actually be capri length (mid-calf) The material is soft - not a hard denim fabric while still giving a denim look. The legs are roomy. Just the way I like them. Ugh! I am not enamored of the "raw hem". It's a raw edge, not a hem. And it's not a raveled edge out of the box, it's simple a cut edge. As I wash each time, I'll observe the ravel situation -- I'm not thrilled that it throws off little threads hither and yon. (minus 2 stars) Either finish it with an actual hem;or a stitched single line slightly above the raw cut. The way it is now is simply a straight unfinished cut across the fabric. The price-point is too high in my estimation for an unfinished raw cut that ultimately throws threads. The vender/manufacturer (whichever) calls it a rustic edgy look. I call it a way to cut manufacturing costs on offering a finished product. I would not give them as a gift, the concept feels low-quality to me -- which offsets their positive attributes.

I am 5'10'' tall, weigh 175 pounds and I ordered a size XL. I adore these wide-leg capris. Lots of stretch which make these so comfy to wear. They measure around 5 inches from the middle of my ankle to the bottom of the pants. But keep in mind I am sorta tall. They look nice if I am wearing a shoe with heels or if I am wearing flat sandals. I think they will look good in the winter with tall boots as well. I consider them to be a medium weight but not so heavy that I can't wear them in the summer. Maybe not on our 90° high humidity days but on most normal summer days. Honestly, I would like a pair in every color. Note: I love the fit on me and I wanted to give them a 5-star review. Unfortunately, I found a small (about an inch) hole in one of the front seams that wasn't sewn completely. See photo. I'm not upset and I'm sure it was an honest mistake with quality control. And it only took me a few minutes to sew it up. So, hence the 4-star review instead of 5-star review.

Online Jeans that fit awesome! Trying to find jeans that fit is hard enough at stores and boutiques. Finding jeans online that actually fit and flatter!! Who knew it existed! I am 5'7", 132 lbs, and have a slender/thin body type. I am also a mom of four, and don't have abs. I typically wear a 27 inch waist pants, and I ordered these in Size Small using the Size chart posted with the item description. I am shocked I got a quality Jean pants that fit like a glove! I will comment that they don't hug the butt all the way around the bottom, but that is because they are flared capri loose styled. These jeans are so cute. If you are able to find both the style and fit you love, I suggest you buy them in all the colors, they are just so nice. The jean has a little bit of stretch in them, and the pant leg has a raw hem so after a few wears and washes, I anticipate some fraying which is okay! They fit, they flatter, they are nice quality!

Very soft band stays put and helps add a size or two to jeans! I wasn't sure what to expect with this, but took a chance and I will actually be ordering a black one. It is so soft and I don't know how it stays put but it did, all day long. The band itself is a very comfy, very soft material. Reminds me of dry fit type material. It comes with two button extenders too. One short and one long. I am 5'3" and 193 lbs. I am 11 weeks pregnant and this one pair of jeans was a little too tight before my pregnancy and right now are not comfortably button-able. But other than that they still fit. This band allowed me to use the extender and cover the jeans so I didn't feel like anything was showing or falling down. The top tummy band is wide and I had no issues with it falling down at all. The bottom has an elastic under to keep the bottom of the band in place. I was concerned with the top falling down that at one point I flipped it, but it honestly works the best as it was intended! Trust the product! :D

Only okay… My measurements: 5’4”, 38DD, 40” waist, 48” hips, 32” inseam. The XXL barely works. The bib hits just over the curve of my boobs, leaving the buttons for the straps just above nipple level - which looks real weird under a cardigan or flannel shirt (see photo). If I were to hike up the straps, I’d have a horrific camel toe. The top of the bib also gaps out weirdly? I don’t think these were made for people with a tummy, at all. The length was perfect for me, but again - I’m only 5’4”…and I wanted to cuff them anyway. The stretch and comfort are the only things going for these. But the thinness of the fabric also means that I’ll never use them for real work.

Definitely loose fit. Medium weight and very soft. The VETINEE Women’s Denim Loose Fit Overalls are definitely loose fit. There is no need to size up as a precaution like I did. Being used to clothing running a size smaller than expected when ordering online, even when ordering by specific measurements, I ordered one size up as a precautionary measure. This time, I sincerely wish I hadn’t! The fabric is medium weight and nice quality. It’s soft and very flexible. The color is exactly as advertised (at least the deep blue I ordered is). These overalls are definitely durable enough for ranch and farm. The XXL fit me excessively baggy and unflattering. Based on my measurements, XL would have been an adequately loose fit. I believe the ankle hem is at 34” for the inseam on these coveralls. They were very long. My measurements (in inches) are: Shoulder to shoulder: 17 Bust: 44 Rib Cage: 35 Waist: 34 Arm Pit to Hip bone: 19 Cloth Length (neck baseline to just below hip bone): 23 Hips: 41 Mid-Thigh: 22 Calf: 14 Inseam: 32 Collarbone Hollow to floor: 55 Dress Size: US Women’s size 12-14 or size L-XL usually. Hope this helps!

The Vetinee Women's Overalls Denim Loose Fit Straight Leg Bib Overall Jeans are the cutest and most comfy bib jeans I have ever owned and that’s saying a lot. I’ve been a bib jeans wearer for many years. As an artist and creator, bib jeans are the best to wear when working and creating. These bib jeans have some nice stretch going on, so no matter what position you’re in, sitting or standing, they are comfortable. The great thing is, they don’t continue to stretch out throughout the day. They maintain their shape and fit, nicely. According to the manufacturer’s size chart and my body measurements, I should have purchased a XXL but I did not. I went with an XL instead so the fit on me isn’t as baggy as on the model in the listing’s photographs. I wasn’t looking for baggy. I have a few pairs of those already. That said, I’m quite happy with the fit and I do love these bib jeans! For sizing reference: My Bust measures 43", My Waist Measures 38", and my Hips Measure 48". I’m 5’5” tall. I ordered the XL.

I've always wanted a pair of overalls so I was excited to find these and try them. I love them and don't know why I waited so long to get a pair. The fabric on these is really comfortable. Though it has the look of denim, it is a soft denim with a mix of cotton, polyester and spandex. The spandex makes them stretchy and the cotton makes them soft. These fit perfectly. I ordered a medium and usually wear a size 6-8. I'm 5'3" and the length is a bit long but I was planning on rolling them anyway so it's not a big deal. The best part about these is I don't have to worry about bending over and having my whole back side, including my butt crack show while I'm doing yard work. And they look cute for wearing around too without working in them. Overall, I can't find anything I don't like and would recommend them for anyone who is looking for a comfortable pair of overalls.

Super cute I’m 5’5” 147 lbs 38D and got these denim overalls in a small. The fit is spot on (I prefer something a bit more fitted), the length is just right for someone my height. The fabric is about medium weight, a bit thinner than Levi’s overalls. But I actually prefer something a bit thinner like these overalls because it’s more comfortable. Large side pockets, fits my iPhone 13 Pro Max. Looks great with a t-shirt or cami. I have to take 1 button off in order to take these on/off because my hips are pretty large. The fabric is easy care, I just wash it like my denim jeans. I prefer to air dry but do like to put it in the dryer for a few minutes after it’s fully dried to soften it up. At the current listed price of $40, I think it’s a pretty reasonable price. Good quality.

Super stretchy! I am 5’7”, 165 lbs, and typically an 8 in bottoms (more for the hips than the waist). I got these in a large after much deliberation and I am glad I did because these fit perfectly. Amazon did suggest I get a medium based on data they had, and honestly the size chart was leaning me towards a medium as well. But I know how shorts can fit me, so I definitely went up a size and I am glad I did. These fit very comfortably and are snug but not too tight anywhere. They feel just like leggings but in shorts, so they are very stretchy. They do ride up a bit, so I could see that getting annoying to have to constantly pull them down all the time. All in all a good pair of shorts, I could see me styling these with oversized long shirts and tunics. For a comfy but cute fit!

The denim blue dress which I ordered in a small and medium were almost identical in size length and material. The medium was about a quarter of an inch wider than the small and that was the only difference that I found. So both ran super big on me. Lastly, I ordered the black denim dress. And it’s a small. Aaaaamzing!!!! This is seriously the most amazing pair of jeans! To start, I live in the Northeast where winters are long and cold. i have owned a few different pair of flannel-lined jeans that I paid MUCH more for than the listed price of these jeans. It's early but my initial opinion is that I like these better. When I took them out of the package, I thought they looked small so I tried them on right away. I'm generally a size 12 with most of my weight around the middle. I ordered large and they fit perfectly. Instant love! I wore them all day. There's just enough stretch to them to accommodate the places where it's needed without being baggy in places where you don't. At 5'4", I'm on the shorter side and the length is perfect for me. Note that they may be a little short on a taller person. The lining is soft and warm but not overly heavy so that you can transition from outdoors to indoors without overheating. The zipper and the button are sturdy and well constructed. One of my other favorite qualities of these jeans is the boot cut. It's logical that if you are wearing flannel-lined pants that you would also be wearing a bootie/boot yet other high end brands of flannel lined jeans typically have a straight or tapered leg which prevents that. This is one of the BEST items that I have ordered online!

Perfect jeggings and true to size Oh WOW these are amazing and I’m already scanning for a second pair. I am 5’3” 117 lb with a 25” waist and 35/36 hip. Based on the size chart, I’m a Small, but I ordered both S and M, both in 7/8 length, based on reviews saying they run small. I tried on the S first and it’s a PERFECT fit; I didn’t even need to try on the M. Fit exactly how I wanted, no waist gape, and SUPER comfortable. Back pocket placement is also perfect for enhancing glutes. They’re flatteringly tight without feeling restrictive like real jeans do. I love that there is a button and belt loops so they look more like real pants and not leggings, as getting away with wearing comfy pants to work is key. My experience is true to size based on my body type and true to color. I’d say more but I need to go shop for a second pair of these!

Lightweight denim I love the design of this 2 pc denim set by Fisoew. The 100% cotton denim is soft, breathable with the perfect amount of stretch and weight. The color is a crisp white with no yellow, which automatically cheapens anything white in my experience. The halter vest top is cool without being too revealing and the wide legged pants are everything. I don’t often buy a lot of clothing online, but I must say this is one of my favorite designs. I love the shape of both the vest and bottoms and was hoping the fit would be exactly like it fits the model in the photo. For size references, I’m 5’6 120 lbs, usually a small, and probably pretty close to the size of the model. The problem is, I referred to the reviews for size guidance. Now at the time I ordered, the majority of the reviews stated concerns about the set being totally too small. So guess what, I sized up! Now I don’t remember ordering a large, but I probably did and that’s what I received. Well, it’s huge and based on the large, even the medium would have been too large. If I had stuck to my regular size small, it would have been perfect. I should have relied on my body type and the fact that both pieces were loose fitting. Nonetheless, I appreciate the ease and comfort of this set. It’s a great look, value and is well crafted ……..🌱Happy to recommend …

Finding the Perfect Fit: A Review of the White 2-Piece Denim Set Let me share my experience with this white 2-piece denim set—it's a tale of size surprises and durable style. As someone who stands at 5'9" but typically orders larger sizes to avoid pants being too short, I opted for a size large. The result? Well, the pant length is spot-on, but the fit elsewhere is, shall we say, roomy. The waist and top are especially loose, thanks to my underestimation of the size and fit. But let's talk about the positives. The material is top-notch—durable, non-transparent, and heavyweight. Plus, the solid stitching and hems throughout ensure longevity and quality. Despite the sizing hiccup, I can't deny the potential of this outfit. It's stylish, well-made, and versatile. My advice? Order the right size for a flawless fit. With the correct sizing, I have no doubt this set would be a wardrobe staple. So, if you're in the market for a chic denim ensemble, don't hesitate to give this one a try—just be sure to get your sizing spot-on for the perfect look and feel.

VERY stretchy! Very long! I’m 5’8, 175lbs, measurements 42-32-41, I’m wearing the size 10 jeans. I was a little worried looking at the size chart and ordering a size 10 as my hips and waist both measured too big, but I took a risk. These jeans definitely run bigger than the sizing chart indicates because I probably could have easily fit an 8. The jeans are incredibly stretchy. They are also SUPER long, I’m wearing a 2” heal in this pic on top of already being 5’8. Tall girls rejoice! The color “nostalgic blue” is a very dark wash, with fading done on the thighs. These jeans are extremely high waisted. I’m not sure that these are my new favorite jeans, but they are cute… just wish I had ordered my usual size, which is an 8, and not used the sizing chart which doesn’t seem accurate.

Very soft and comfortable. I love these jeans. They have a high waist without being uncomfortable. Due to them being wide leg jeans and me being 5’4”, I definitely need to wear a shoe with a heel of some sort, unless I want to trip all over myself. The fabric is very soft and has a good stretch and I loved the feel from the moment I put them on. Great pair of jeans, but fair warning, if you’re vertically challenged, be ready to wear the jeans with heels.

Tall Girl Friendly I love that these jeans are made for long legs. I have a 32.5" inseam and these jeans reach all the way to my ankles. The jeans are comfy and the material is nice. The con for me is that these jeans aren't truly wide leg. They are like a weird cross between straight and wide leg. When I want a wide leg, I like it to be very noticeable and these just look more like straight leg or bootcut. And another con is even though I have a short torso, I usually find high waist jeans look well on me. These don't. the crotch hangs too low (the rise is too long) and they are baggy between the waist and hip area. They are just not flattering on me without the top of these jeans being covered. I ordered according to the waist size on the size chart but I'm guessing I should have gotten my regular size 0/2. I ordered the size 4 and I am 5'10, 118 lbs, 32-25-34 for reference. If you are looking more for a bootcut jean and you have a really long rise then these jeans may just work out well for you.

The perfect boyfriend cut jean Loose throughout the leg and gets softer and looser as you wear them, exactly as should be in a boyfriend cut jean. It is more like traditional denim, there is not a lot of elastic in the material, but the denim is high quality and softens up A LOT. With the loser leg fit, you don’t need the stretch. These photos show wear after 8 hours and how much they can loosen up! Size down one size. I probably could have gone down 2 sizes except that boyfriend cut jeans are supposed to fit loser in the hips and legs in general so it doesn’t make sense going too tight. I’m usually at 27, at 5’3” and usually 128lbs with curves in hips and thighs, but the 26 fit perfectly. Bonus points: they are very comfy while pregnant! I’m currently 5 months pregnant too and , while my waist size hasn’t changed drastically yet the 26 is still the right size. Since there is a tiny bit of stretch material in them, I probably have easily another inch around the waist I can grow into if pushed.

Good quality but to short These are super cute except the fact the waist is elastic band and I'm not tall but they are to short on me to wear with boots I pulled them down a little bit then the top doesn't look right because of the elastic band I think they would be good with flats or sandals I'm 5'4

Long and comfy! Loved these pants! I bought them for my bachelorette party. They are really comfortable and stretchy. I was really happy that they were long! Im about 5’10 140lbs and got a size 8 and they went to the floor. Will definitely be wearing them again.

Tall plus Girl Okay so first off I didn’t pay for expedited and it still came the next day! When it says earliest delivery date, most times Amazon pushes through and it’s COVID-19 so I’m shocked. I am 6 foot I weigh 280 pounds and I’m thick thighs no butt. For the thick thighs women with no butt is best to get your exact size because we want to also flatter our butts just as much as are thighs. If your thighs are bigger than your butt or vice versa to an extreme amount probably get one size bigger but it may be a little loose. I like tighter fit jeans in my thigh area to complement my hips and thighs also because I have a small butt once again lol! And you also know the more you wear jeans they stretch. So I know that my thighs will start to make room for themselves if I need any more LOL.These are perfect. They stretch but you can tell There’s some denim in it because it doesn’t have an extreme stretch. These are not jeggings! They are the regular polyester plus size material for big girls I just have jeans are being right now with a little stretch but I still denim feel! I don’t mind that lip space under the abdomen because I know my thighs are the reason why! Also it goes all the way down ! No high water !!!!

Love them but elastic is off My first darker Jean pair was perfect. They are so comfy and flattering. I am 5’6”, 155lbs with wider hips/butt and the Medium fit perfectly. I returned to by the orange and then the black. The orange elastic doesn’t stay in place. It constantly folds over on itself. I’m constantly fussing with it to open it back up. I have the black on today for the first time and I can’t tell if the elastic is folded over or it’s just too thin. Something is definitely off. I added a picture for reference. These pants are not expensive but they also are not dirt cheap. I expect better quality for $35. Esp when you can get decent quality jeans at Target for $20.

Almost perfect I like how stretchy the material is. Thick enough. I love the flare as well. For your reference: I am 5'2", US Medium. 160 lbs. Somethings can be improved in the design. Adding some side pockets would be really nice because it would really make it look like real pants instead of jeggings with elastic waistband. I kind of like to tuck my shirt in my pockets. The waistband is a bit too tight. These are high-waist pants and it being so tight around the waist is restricting because I have GERD it messes up my stomach because it pushes my stomach too much. For that reason I can't wear it as much as I want to. If the waist band is at least 2 inches loose, these would be comfortable. I like that the hemline is raw so I was able to cut these to match my height and not worry about sewing. Because I am short, the hole is not hitting me at the knees, but it doesn't bother me. The only thing I really would say are not very good at not having pockets and the waistline being super tight. Other than that, I would have give these 5 stars because they are cute! If you are tall and skinny, you would love these!

Fits perfect 😍 Perfect - the stretch, length, material, EVERYTHING… except the knee holes I have to fix and make a tad wider. I’d relate them to leggings but a little more tighter. I originally sized up to an XXL but as you see the first two pictures (with the pink shirt) the waist band was way too lose and very baggy around my tummy. The second set of pictures (with the red shirt) I ordered my normal size XL and they fit PERFECT. These will hands down be my go to! For reference, I wear 14-16 in jeans and my measurements are roughly 47” hips 40” waist.

Buy the jeans! Super cute and fit just right! The stretch in the waistband covers and sucks in my mama pooch, which makes my waist and butt flattering in these jeans! I’m 5’5”, 160 pounds, & I usually wear a 29/30 waist in jeans. I ordered a medium and they fit spot-on, the length was perfect for if I’m wanting to wear flats or a small wedge. This is after a wash on cold and medium heat dry, which I do with all my jeans. The knee slits aren’t super noticeable unless sitting or kneeling, but I wasn’t worried about the distressing. The downside is if you want front pockets, but I hardly use front pockets so it didn’t break the deal for me. I’m so glad I bought these jeans, worth the money for sure!

Overall nice pants I am 5’5” and 187 lbs for reference. Just took these out of the package today, and the first thing I noticed was some sort of animal hair on both legs. Which has nothing to do with the fit, but is still a little weird to me. I ordered an XL to be safe, and I probably could have gone with the Large because they aren’t as fitted as I would like in the stomach or thighs. So if you are about like me in size and like a more relaxed fit, the XL would be great. I’m hoping I can dry them and shrink them a little. Also the “distressing” (slits in the knees) don’t come up quite high enough because they don’t hit my knees, so maybe I’m not tall enough. But I feel like they’re a good length because I plan to wear heeled booties with them. But overall they’re nice and if I buy the dark denim pair I’ll probably get a Large.

Best jeans ever! LOVE LOVE LOVE!!! I am a big fan of flair/bell bottom jeans. I loved them back in the 90s and early 00s and I still love them. It's irritating that stores don't sell them. I don't care what's in fashion, I care about what I like and how it looks on me. I was a little apprehensive to buy these because of the elastic waist but I did it anyways and bought 2 pair because I need flairs back in my life again. Once I tried them on, the elastic was out of my mind. They are a little long but that's how I like my jeans. I am 5'4" and 162 lbs. and bought a large.. Favorite jeans ever. I will wear them probably every single day this fall and winter season. And spring as well. Probably summer too. Buy them. You won't regret it.

Great quality and fit! These jeans are SO CUTE!!! :-D This was my 1st clothing purchase from Amazon. I was a bit skeptical that the quality would be good. I was so happy to find that the quality is great, and the fit is beautiful. I am 5’5, 137 lbs, and my waist is 27 inches, my hips are 36 inches. I ordered a size small and found that it is true to size. I love how extremely comfortable these pants are. They have a nice stretch throughout. I will definitely wear these often. I am tempted to also get them in a lighter color, but some people in the reviews have said that the lighter colored jeans aren’t the same quality as the dark ones, so I will give more thought to that.

Super soft and stretchy These pants were super comfortable and stretchy. The elastic waist band fit well over my belly however, I felt the Bs l side was shorter than the belly side. I had to fold the top of the front of the pants down. I’m 5’5” and the length was perfect. I bought size 18 and it fit true to size.

Love them! These are so comfortable! I normally have trouble finding pants that fit right but I read a LOT of reviews on these before I purchased. I have wanted to purchase a pair of bell bottom for a while and was nervous about this purchase from Amazon. I'm so happy i got them. Great purchase. I highly recommend. When I first tried these jean I instantly love the fit. My hubby even gave me a compliment on them. I have hips/stomach and these pants made me look slimmer. I'm 5'4", 187 lbs and the jeans are a size large. I plan on ordering the white and blue pair net.

Bigger than 2xl don’t bother So the jeans showed up damaged but that’s not even the issue really my issue is anything above a 2xl doesn’t have prime so it took 6 weeks to get to me which is insane. Then it got here damaged and instead of being able to return it by the UPS ppl scanning a QR code I now have to go to a staples and print the shipping label (bc who tf owns a printer anymore) and then mail it out which will more than likely take at least a week before I get my money back so we are looking at almost 2 months of them having my money for nothing. I also ordered a size 2xl with prime and I love them but I refuse to make my rating higher because of it simply on the principal that yet again another company is inconveniencing ppl because they’re fat. Same fatphobia different company, smh. So yeah if you’re above a 2xl or bellow 170lb don’t waste your time. Side note I see a lot of stick thin women giving these pants bad reviews because they’re “baggy” on them that is because they are elastic waist with stretch they are meant for big girls if you want cute bell bottoms that will fit you get classic denim with a button closure! I’m 203lbs and 5” 2’ and the 2XL fits great

These. Jeans. Are. EVERYTHING. 5’5, 180lbs, 5 months postpartum. Ordered a Large. I am very Getting to love my post baby body, that includes wider hips than pre-baby, and these jeans make it so easy. They are so stretchy and comfy but they are snug in all the right places. They are a tad baggy in the crotch area for me, but I think one wash might shrink them up just a little. Also, they are LONG on me, however. A heeled bootie fixed that problem and I am obsessed. Just buy them.

Pricey but worth it! Bellbottoms are back! These are very cute! Super cute. I got so many compliments. There are holes at the knees. I overlooked that in the description. Just FYI. Holding up after a year of use, but not warm I’m writing this review approximately one year after I purchased them for hiking and running in colder weather. After a year of use, they are still holding up - no rips or tears. Unfortunately, after the first wash on gentle cycle, I noticed some mild pilling, which only continued with further washes. They look pretty worn now. I found these to be less warm than other fleece-lined tights, so I would recommend them for temperatures no less than 45 degrees Fahrenheit. However, I do love the fit and color. I am 5’3 and these aren’t baggy at the bottom.

As shown in the site I recently purchased the Vetinee Jean Overalls for Women in a loose fit, stretchy denim jumpsuit, and overall, I’m very satisfied with the product. The fit is fine; I usually wear a medium but decided to order a larger size, and it turned out bigger as I expected. At 5’4", the length is a bit longer for me, but it’s manageable. The color is exactly as shown on the website, which is great. The jeans have Lycra, making them very comfortable to wear. They arrived rolled in a plastic bag, which was fine for me. One thing I didn’t like is that the white part of the pants shows, and I wish they had used denim for the entire inner pocket. Despite this, the product is well-made and meets my expectations.

So-so… I am a big fan overalls, so I was excited for these. Unfortunately, the probably will not become a best-loved, well-worn piece of clothing for me. Why is that? First of all based on the size chart, I sized up. I shouldn’t haven done that and probably should have ordered my regular size (for reference I am an L, and I ordered an XL). They ended up being baggier than I prefer. Another issue with these is that they are not for short girls, which is great if you’re a tall lass. But I am not quite 5’3” and these were super long on me. I included 2 photos, one with the cuffs rolled and the other without. I didn’t love the way they looked with the cuffs rolled, but I was not about to have my pants dragging like this was the 90s, you know? The final issue I had was the color. The ones I got are supposed to be a light pink color. The ended up being more orange (maybe you can see that my nail polish is orange and the overalls seem to match. Anyway, I didn’t love these as much as I thought I would, so with all that said, I will leave it up to you whether or not to get these.

Sturdy & flattering, run small I'm fond of these jeans. I love a straight leg and I love a patch pocket, so I was already primed to like them. I was a little apprehensive about them being only 85% cotton, but they feel like true denim to me (unlike some more polyester-y feeling jeans.) The "classic blue" color really is a classic. I love that they're a solid wash without any whiskering or fading (which I tend to despise.) I only wish they had a little more slackness in the thighs, because they do cling to the tops of my thighs a little bit, which is typically not something that happens with a straight leg. But it's not too bad; I'm probably my own worst critic where my upper thighs are concerned. They hold my tummy pooch in rather than emphasizing it, which is nice. I am a size 10 with wide hips, and ordered the large. The waistband is snug, so these definitely run on the small side. I'm 5'5" and the hem falls just above my heel in the back. I usually prefer an ankle length, but these jeans are the perfect full length, which is better for wintertime anyway. I think they will be a smidge more comfortable after I lose just a couple pounds, but I am pleased with these jeans and anticipate wearing them a lot in the months to come.

I sized down from a 9 to a 7 and they fit well. Just wish indigo was darker I am currently a solid Women’s size 8, Juniors size 9 (W29.5, H36, Ht 5’3.5) and wear the Hybrid & Co super stretch twill pants in Medium. After reading the reviews for the Indigo Jeans I purchased one size smaller in a 7 and they fit perfectly (these are offered in Juniors sizing, so that translates to a Womens size 6). I have a 29” inseam and these are long enough, and more than reach the top of my vans. They button at my natural waist and fit nice and tight everywhere without pinching or causing “muffin top” My only two issues are: 1. Although they fit snugly at first, they don’t offer much shaping or hold. They are very comfortable, but stretch out pretty quick in the hips and knees just from driving. I cannot image I would fit into a size 5, so I don’t think this can be remedied by sizing down again. 2. Indigo is advertised here as a much darker color, and most denim companies market “indigo” as their darkest shade before black. This Indigo denim is not dark or sleek unfortunately. It is a medium, work-a-day blue. I like it enough to keep them but have posted pics so that others can decide before buying 🤗🙂

Really well made! I think sizing is a little tricky… I’ve been looking for a pair of white jeans for awhile, so went for these - my 1st pair of trendy wide leg pants. I did wash them before wearing because there were deep fold lines + I wanted to see if they shrank because of the 75% cotton (they did a little bit in the length). WHAT I LIKE: == very comfortable! The stretch of the fabric softens them. == the button fly is flattering & flattening. == the length ends at my ankle bones, so your shoes are featured. WHAT I DON’T LIKE: == the wide legs need ironing in the opposite direction, if that makes sense. They flare out oddly at the very bottom. MY SIZE/THESE PANTS: I’m 5’8”, 108 lbs, & wear size 2/4. I ordered these in size 4 & might have needed a 2 instead. They’re plenty roomy & the 23% polyester/2% spandex makes them stretch out as you wear them, so they get a bit looser throughout the day/evening. Bottom line? They’re a really well made pair of denim pants in a trendy style. Pay attention to the size chart & order carefully. **Thank you for reading this review & I hope that it’s !** 😊

I am an overalls person and I love this. I’ve been wanting one that’s relaxed fit and this is exactly that. It’s comfortable and can be worn with any top. Great spring and summer wear. The material of the denim feels nice. It’s more on the softer denim and easy to wear all day. I ordered a medium and I like the way it fits on me and it’s more relaxed than fitted. I am 5’3” and weight 130 lbs for size reference. I recommend sizing up if your wanting an oversize and relax fit. For my height, the length is about 2 inches long, but I like the baggy look so I’m happy there is extra length plus when I wear tennis shoes, it’ll be perfect. Straps are adjustable so you can always adjust the length as well. Love the pockets in the front. It does give vintage vibe. Can’t wait to wear it when the weather gets warmer. I recommend if you’re looking for overalls.

Not For Petites. Man, I was so excited for these ETOSELL Baggy Jeans, but sadly they are just not made for us Petites! I am 5'0 & 115 lbs (with a 28" waist & 40" hips) and usually wear a size 6, so that is what I ordered. These here jeans are just way too long for my short self and are also kind of too big everywhere else. I really loved the look of these jeans and even own another pair of ETOSELL Cargo Jeans in a size 6, which fit me really well, so yeah I was definitely disappointed that the fit of these were so off compared to my other pair from this brand. The jeans are made well, they are heavy, do not have stretch to them, but are comfortable. I would not recommend these jeans if you are on the shorter side like myself. I would possibly consider sizing down since these are so baggy? Maybe they would have fit a little better if I got a size 4, not totally sure though...just a thought!

The good, the bad and the rest Heavy weight denim used for these so should hold up. I chose the Dark Blue | Size: X-Large. For reference I'm over 50 years old. Height 5'6", weight 170lbs measurements Bust: 43 Waist: 34 Hips: 43. The fabric on these is better than I was expecting. No stretch at all, but baggy enough not to interfere with movements. I also like that the color is uniform. I don't care for the worn out faded in patches look. The inseam is about 29.5". The stitching and construction all seem solid. And the front pockets are nice and deep, can even hold my kindle 7. The crotch fit is a little weird on me, but that's due to my shape. You're experiences will probably be different. In general, these are good jeans. Peace and sturdy pants

Poor quality, didn't love the fit I am 5'2" and hippy/curvy and have a bit of a butt. These pants fit true to size when following the chart, but I found that the crotch area was very unflattering and only wear these with a top that will cover the front. It puckers a bit, which makes me wonder if they're not more flattering on someone with a slimmer/straighter build than mine. Also out of the package, my jeans had damage on the seam that goes down the front of the legs. You can see it in the photos I provided. For the price, I find that kind of quality issue to be unacceptable. All things considered the quality of these jeans felt like something that shouldn't be more than half the price listed, in my opinion. I wouldn't recommend these if you're looking for a well-made quality product.

What to Expect Bitttccch (you're not a bitch, I'm just using it as a expression) if you haven't bought these pants then you need to. Now I've always had a problem with finding my size-- I always bought too big or too small. I am 5"5, 194 lbs w/ the dimensions of 33-42-25 (waist-hip-thigh). I ordered the size 14 and it fits me snugly, however not tightly bc its got enough stretch for you to pop a very wide street squat, do a high kick, etc. They are long so you can roll em up but that's ok. They are soft material and they don't feel restricting. You should know your measurements before buying if you are not sure what size in jeans you are, you can even ask if you aren't sure what size to get. But if not, try going with your normal size

I’m liking these! Fit me well. I like these Dokotoo cargo-style hiking shorts more than I thought I would. They fit me pretty well, and I like that the waistband is tie-style, it’s stretchy and adjustable. The material itself has some stretch and give, it isn’t completely stiff but don’t expect it to stretch a whole size or anything. For reference, I’m about 130lbs at 5’5”, and my measurements are 36”/28”/38”. I usually wear a size 8 in pants, though I recently lost some weight so I might fit a 6. Medium works well on me! The pant legs are a little larger than my thighs, but I’d rather have them be a bit too big over too small. The only things I’m not as thrilled about is the fact the pockets on the back aren’t real (but the front pockets are and nice and roomy!), and that the care instructions seem difficult. The label says it must be hand-washed cold, and can’t be dry cleaned or tumble dried, and to cool iron if needed. I’m not sure who really has time to hand wash their clothes, but it isn’t me! However I did throw them in my washer on a regular cycle with the rest of my laundry (on cool temp water) and they seemed to come out just fine. I hung them up to dry and it did seem like they wrinkled more than I would like, but that’s nothing an iron can’t handle. Overall I’m liking these shorts and will continue to test and wear them over the summer! So far they’re very comfortable and trendy. I recommend them and hope they will last me at least for the next few seasons!

Best maternity jeans for this prize, fit like a glove!! First things first, I normally wear 12-14 jean size and I'm 5′ 2″ tall and weigh 190lbs but I have grown in belly size so hope this helps others choose your size. (I bought the Light Wash3 in size 1X) I am now 5 months pregnant so my normal day-to-day jeans won't budge. These jeans are the first jeans that have fit me without constricting my body, belly or legs got some great stretch to them and also don't drag past my feet, they're perfect length. Not to mention they're not baggie on me making them a lot more flattering than most maternity pants I've worn. I am extremely shocked with how perfect these jeans fitted me and how amazing i felt in them, for $20 dollars these are the best jeans I've owned in my opinion, even though some say they're cheap looking or feel cheap, not to me NOT FOR 20 dollars! High end maternity jeans are way over 50-100 dollars nowadays so these are great for my budget. The belly band is long enough it reaches right below my bra and is very stretchy but not loose, I still have 4 months to go so i hope they'll fit then. I will make sure to buy different colors and update this review accordingly. Now for the negatives (not a huge deal breaker but important to note) These jeans have no front pockets, they're fake pockets which was a disappointment but for some dumb reason women pants normally don't have pockets so im used to it (I wish they would add pockets though, please do!) Another negative is the color is a bit different from the model, mine were a bit lighter in color but honestly not a huge deal for me. Third negative for me is that I expected them to have a hem at the end like in the picture of the model but also something that isn't a huge deal. Lastly, they did have a strong chemical smell like some reviews have mentioned, but the smell reminds me of mothballs so I assume they use some kind of moth control to keep their jeans protected, after I washed them the scent faded away. All in all, these negatives are not a deal breaker whatsoever, so I will definitely get more!

Definitely wide leg baggy. I ordered these PLNOTME Women’s Boyfriend Wide Leg Jeans for a healthy purpose. I have osteoarthritis in my knees and plan to wear my new knee braces when my boyfriend and I start walking trails regularly during the cool fall weather. I needed jeans that would allow room for my braces. These jeans are definitely 12" wide leg baggy.I ordered the largest size 14 (size chart measurements: waist 36.6", hip 46.5") because my waist is 38" and hips are 42". Lucky for me, the waist and hips fit well. Other reviewers have said they run small. The waist band is also adjustable with additional buttonholes. I stand 5'4" and already knew these would be long on me, as are most jeans I have ordered. Sometimes, I can roll up pantlegs, but these wide legs won't accommodate. The fabric is not overly heavy, and the stitching appears good. Because they are wide-legged, I was able to slide these on even while wearing my sneakers, and no one will know when I wear knee braces underneath. I did notice a bit of a buckling with the zipper though, which was even more apparent after I washed them.I checked to make sure these jeans were colorfast before I washed them in cold water on a delicate cycle. Then I tumble-dried on medium heat with other jeans but removed and hung them 15 minutes before the end of the cycle. I wore my white shirt that's more form-fitting. I didn't notice any shrinkage in the jeans, other than maybe a half inch in the length. However, the zipper’s buckling was definitely more uneven. I figured out it's better to zip up, first, and then button the waist.Overall, this wide leg style doesn't really look attractive on me because of my apple shape and short height, but these PLNOTME Women’s Boyfriend Wide Leg Jeans will serve a purpose for me to start walking again for exercise so I can start losing this excessive weight.

Soft flowy denim through legs, tight waist. I will preface this by saying I am 5’4” & short-waisted, but these are not mid rise, but high waisted. While the leg is full & flowy (for denim), the waist is very fitted. I am between a 10-12 depending on fit, I always size up to be safe when ordering online. These fit but are tight through the waist. People slimmer than me won’t have this issue. If you are in between sizes, definitely size up. The denim is nice, but no stretch. I feel like a little spandex is put in a lot of pants these days, these are listed as 48% Cotton, 36% Rayon, 16% Polyester, so no give. The model has a cuff in the listing, but the material isn’t stiff enough to hold a cuff. If you want a cuff, extra steps will be necessary. As I am short, I was looking to cuff too. They are a smidge long on me, but not as much as I thought they would be, so they may be short in taller women. That might be a cute look, as long as you know what you’re getting. Love the patch pockets, but I do wish they didn’t have the buttons on the waist band, as I would like to wear with a belt sometimes. Overall, a cute look that is something a little different.

OBSESSED!!! UPDATE 2: I have been wearing and washing these at least 1-2x a week and they’ve held up great so I purchased another pair and it will be delivered today. 🥰 I was soooo worried a L/XL wouldn’t fit me because I’ve never had luck with apparel that’s meant to fit multiple sizes, but these are wonderful!! So glad I took the chance! I am somewhere between a 14 and a 16 depending on style and brand. These are buttery soft as claimed and very stretchy. I had my husband check if they are see through by doing squats and bends in the sun and he says no. They fit great in every way, including length. I have a 32” inseam and they hit right at my ankle. I do have a very short torso and the waist band sits a few inches above my bellybutton almost to my chest but that is the same with every high rise anything ever. 🙃 The stomach band is wide but I wouldn’t consider these tummy controlling at all, but the thick/wide band does keep them in place. They are fantastic and I will be purchasing an additional pair. I love them so much I wanted to post my review right away but I will come back with an update after they’ve been washed and dried. UPDATE: Washed these leggings on a regular cycle with my other clothes and dried them normally. They are in the same condition and size as they were when they went in. So glad they didn’t shrink at all and the rips are not stretched, distorted, or changed in anyway.

True high waisted joggers that fit comfortably! These joggers are keepers. I'm 5’7”, 135 lb, curvy on the bottom half with about 28” waist and 38” hips. I struggle to find “high waisted” pants that actually cover my belly button and joggers that don’t end up as high waters. These joggers absolutely hit the mark on those points. I usually wear small, occasionally medium. Ordered these in small, and they fit with plenty of room to spare. I see people saying that the elastic digs into their waists. These joggers are a high-waisted fit. They are supposed to cover your belly button and fit at the narrowest point of your waist. If you try to wear them lower, the elastic will dig into you. So wear them at the point they were intended to be worn. Someone else said these were men’s pants—I would disagree. These are women’s joggers, but there is “slight” pleating on the front, especially if the interior drawstring is drawn tight for a small waist. This produces some bagginess in the front. These joggers look best with a cropped top or sports bra. They are VERY comfortable! The fleece lining is very light. I wouldn’t wear these in 90-100 degree temps, but they could easily be worn mid-70s down to much colder temps. Bought these on sale and overall, I’m satisfied. Finally I have high waisted pants that actually reach my belly button and comfortably cover my ankles. I will get great use out of these pants majority of the year.

Comfortable! These pants are not what I expected, because the fabric is shiny. However, I think they’ll be fine for walks on chilly days. The pants are well made and I like the soft fleece lining. I am 5’8” and 132lbs. and the size Medium, 32” inseam is a little large, but I think they’ll work.

Nice pants! Be aware of the characteristics to make sure they're for you Overall, awesome pants! Pros: - Long enough! Probably even too long (I got large tall) but as a tall girl who rarely finds long enough inseams, I have a tendency to overdo it just to relish in the rare experience of too much length. - super comfy. The outside is smooth fabric and the inside is a thin, soft fleece. Warm enough for cool weather but not so insulated that they're a deep-winter-only pair. - pockets are deep enough, ankles are snug enough to look good without being tight Cons: - the sizing chart was a bit wonky, so out of caution I sized up to a large knowing I'd rather be comfy than sausage-cased. There's a lot of extra room, so I really should have stuck to the usual medium. For reference, I'm 5'10", 160lbs, and typically wear size 8 pants. I have a 33" inseam. - the outside is kinda shiny. Coupled with the extra material, they feel a little like 80's windbreaker pants without the awful swishing sounds The photos are done after washing and drying to see if there was any shrinkage (anyone how has felt the disappointment of bringing home pants that were long enough in the store to end up having yet another pair of slightly too short house pants after the first wash knows why I waited to review) and I didn't detect any shrinking, so that's fantastic for buying true to size. These pants have me excited for autumn weather so I can get cozy in these pants that'll actually keep my ankles warm!

Knee bagging and wrinkles I used to have a high opinion of the Gap brand, but my perspective has changed. I’m 5'4" and 150 lbs, and I ordered a medium, which fit me well. I liked it when I first wore it, even though the material felt thin. However, after just 4-5 wears, the pants started developing knee bagging. It’s also constantly wrinkled when I want to wear it, which is frustrating. The back pockets were sewn shut, but I managed to open them myself. I originally bought these for comfortable wear, but with the baggy knees and constant wrinkles, I lose confidence every time I put them on. Don’t buy these, there are better options out there

Do not size up… Size reference: I am 5’6” and 126 lbs. Almost all of my jeans are US size 4 or 27 and the majority of my elastic waist pants are medium (leggings, jeggings, joggers, sweats, etc.). The size chart suggested a small for sizes 4 and 6 but I decided to order my stretchy pant size of medium. Because of the cotton and rayon fabric, I decided to prepare for a little shrinkage. That was a mistake. Material: Thin, buttery soft, with just a hint of stretch. Lovely shade of army green with an elastic waist, functioning front hip pockets, and drawstring. Opinion: Definitely go by the size chart and downsize if you are on the line. These pants are so large on me that I can’t even cinch them with the drawstring to stay on. Way too big in the waist and baggy in the rear and legs. Honestly, the small probably would be extra roomy as well because these are not even close to my other mediums. They seem well made and I love the softness of the material. Definitely not a good choice for snow days but great for warmer weather. If they had fit, I would have used them for business casual attire at the office during the summer.

Too loose fitting in the waist and hips I really wanted to love these pants because I like their style and fabric but unfortunately they look unflattering on me. I’m 5’7” 150 lbs. I have relatively slim hips and thighs. My waistline is my trouble area where I carry most of my extra weight. I usually wear pull-on style pants in size large. These pants in size large are little too big on me. The waistband is too loose even with the adjustable drawstrings. When I walk it keeps loosening and I have to tighten the strings frequently. There’s too much space in the hips. I know these are not supposed to be tight fitting but on the model they fit in a relaxed yet flattering way and me they look frumpy and they make me look thicker as well as shorter than I am. I forgot to take a picture from the back but that’s where they look the worse on me. Also, when I’m sitting there’s too much fabric in the hips level that bunches up unflatteringly in the front. These are not for me but I’m still giving them 4 stars because I think that if you can find a size that fits you correctly, they could actually be nice, casual, breathable, lightweight pants.

Go by hip measurement I ordered a Small PETITE in indigo. I am 5 feet tall and wear a size 4. I was hoping they would fit shorter, however they do hit at ankle. Pants fit loosely, I would say closer to a 6 in the waist (chart says 4-6) but because of drawstring in waistline they can be pulled in. My hip measurement from chart is between a size XS and S but because of the cotton/rayon content I was fearful of shrinkage and did not want them to fit tight as these are a more casual look. They did not shrink to a noticeable degree. Material is on the lighter side for pants, I would consider these better for warmer seasons. Very comfortable and soft to wear. I disagree with some reviews that say pants have stretch. There is no elastic in material, and I don't consider cotton/rayon to be stretchy. Front pockets fit my entire hand (or cell phone), however back pocket is shorter.

Comfy Butt Not Perfect These are extremely comfortable since they are stretch denim, and they're exactly what I was looking for. Since the majority of the reviews mentioned you might want to go up a size - and I normally wear an 11/12 - I went with a 13. I am 5'8" and 145 pounds. They are just a skosh looser than I would like, and I probably could have stuck to my regular size, but the looseness is also to be expected from any stretchy fabric. And I need to breathe. Can't have it both ways. I haven't laundered them yet, so I'm hoping a little time in the dryer may resolve that. Or eating more croissants... Note: I need to mention I am 64, so you youngins may want to ignore this review. I have PTSD from trying on jeans. It has always been a horrible experience. First, I have a slight case of Dunlops disease...which stands for "the belly done lopped over the belt." But the waist on these is higher, so my midriff is under the covers, where it should be. Secondly, I grew up with skinny thighs, so jeans always bag at my thighs, both in front and in back! And with barely a booty to speak of, all the tea in china isn't going to help these jeans "lift" mine. One can't expect miracles if there's nothing to work with. But I have to admit they are a little more firming around the tush than most jeans. There's a slight looseness in the front crotch area that I'm not thrilled with but, again, that could be my lack of padding in my thighs. The length is perfect, though, and overall I'm really pleased with these jeans.

Note: I need to mention I am 64, so you youngins may want to ignore this review. I have PTSD from trying on jeans. It has always been a horrible experience. First, I have a slight case of Dunlops disease...which stands for "the belly done lopped over the belt." But the waist on these is higher, so my midriff is under the covers, where it should be. Secondly, I grew up with skinny thighs, so jeans always bag at my thighs, both in front and in back! And with barely a booty to speak of, all the tea in china isn't going to help these jeans "lift" mine. One can't expect miracles if there's nothing to work with. But I have to admit they are a little more firming around the tush than most jeans. There's a slight looseness in the front crotch area that I'm not thrilled with but, again, that could be my lack of padding in my thighs. The length is perfect, though, and overall I'm really pleased with these jeans.

cute but short cute, fit well.. I'm in between 5'4 and 5'5 and I weigh 119. I wear a 26 in guess jeans and a size 2 from express. my only complaint is that these are short. The picture shows them reaching your ankles but they don't for me.

Nice Quality, but size up I’m actually surprised at the quality of this skirt. It’s a thick denim, not the lightweight stuff – do remember that because this skirt doesn’t flow softly and it might be a little stiff for some. I purchased both the grey and dark blue denim in this style and love them both. The buttons are fixed on well, the seams are good, and they seem like any other pair of well made jeans (other than it's a skirt!). You will most likely want to size up, unless showing you ‘assets’ is something that appeals to you. I’m in my 60’s, and I’m 5’5”, 107 lbs and quite fit. I ordered a size Small. Not Extra Small. I’ve a tiny waist, and this skirt still fits well. But it clearly hugs my butt. Not something I’m keen on in this part of my life. I’m showing it with a cashmere sweater tucked in (it’s rather thin), and also with a sweater worn on the outside. The skirt does look far better when it’s ironed – I washed and ironed both (no, neither skirt shrunk in a warm wash and regular dryer) and they do look far nicer than the photos I’m showing. The bottom of the skirts tend to get stuck in the tops of my taller ankle boots, but not so much in my shorter boots. Again, it’s a stiffer skirt, semi-full, so walking more slowly is required. Well, at least for me! Still, I love my skirts! I think they'll be so nice this winter with a long, warm sweater. I've a pair of fleece lined tights that will look great with either skirt, and keep me warm this winter.

Terrible smell! I purchased the 2xl in Blue. I want to love this jacket, since it fits perfectly and is exactly what I am looking for but the smell is so BAD! It arrived with tags on and in a plastic ziplocked bag. Once I took it out it had a horrid odor, like your face being smushed up to a new stack of tires or like it was dipped in chemicals! I washed it with a brand name quality detergent most of us use and lightly dried it with my favorite smelling dryer sheets. Still it smells bad! I then sprayed it with a wrinkle eraser spray that smells like clean cotton and hung it. After letting it hang to air out for two days it STILL SMELLS! For anyone that can overlook the smelliness, the collar is not frayed and the 2xl is the perfect size for anyone that is a size DD, 18 or 20, and 5'10''+. It has a bit of a stretchiness to it, which is nice. However, it is much lighter denim than the ones you get at some other big brand names and stores.

These GENLECK crossover, high waist, cuffed jeans are so on trend right now! I really like the softness of the denim, good pockets and just enough distressing in the wide legs. My husband doesn't care for what he calls "the high water" look, however, when the trend is over you can clip a couple stitches for a regular fringed length. The waist has the regular button closure and a hidden button underneath. Now the sizing... For reference I'm 5'6" 128# ~ waist 30, hips 36. When I ordered I noticed 2 size charts and went with the one recommended by Amazon for a 30" waist, that chart showed sizing measurements from GENLECK ~ so I went with M. However, that chart had different size options according to style, I missed that originally. The waist sizing was smaller on the chart at the top of the page, but with the stretch (and these have good stretch) I figured M should work. When I got them, I noticed the tags had 2 different sizes labeled S and M! I'm not sure what size I received, doing my own measuring they seem to be the S from the top chart. They really are cute, but watch the sizing...

Comfortable with a Flattering Cut It’s always hit and miss for me to order clothing online, but this GENLECK wide leg capri jeans are a great find. Material is stretchy in all the right places, and the off centered zipper is a cool design feature. I am 5’10 and 165 pounds and the large fit me well, although I likely could have sized down. The denim is on the heavier side, so it feels more structured and durable but still comfy to wear all day. Length is good, fading and scuffs are trendy, and the pockets are ample. They did arrive with a slight denim smell but it disappeared quickly. I’d advise washing before wearing as the color is rich and saturated and I worry that would bleed onto other fabrics.

Warm legs! A little bulky, but manageable. I'm always cold, so I finally started buying more fleece-lined clothes. These keep my legs warm and I am happy that I bought them. I would prefer a longer inseam though! -Sizing- I’m 5’3” and I bought "Elasticwaist Blue88" in medium when I was wearing about an eight in pants, but I couldn't get these on at all! The next winter I was down to a four or six - and these finally fit. Several reviewers said these ran large, but my experience is that the medium runs small. -Washing- These machine wash and dry easily, without any noticeable shrinking. Colorfast on the outside, fluffy warm on the inside. I wash these with my dark clothes. -Wearing- Thick fleece = bulky. The bulk isn’t unreasonable, but it is visible without a jacket. These pants are fairly stretchy, but my issue is the length. Walking is fine, but sitting gets uncomfortable because the pants are really tight on my kneecaps. So I pull these up to get more slack at the knees -which makes driving more comfortable- but then my ankles get cold because they've lost their cover. A longer inseam would be great. Until then I just need to be mindful about socks or boots to compensate for cold ankles at the expense of knee mobility. -Overall- Great buy! I can finally go outdoors in the winter and not freeze immediately. A longer inseam would be nice, but these are still worth buying if you’re always cold like me.

Comfortable, stretches as you wear I bought two different pairs- an xs and a s. The XS was more "fitting" but then the waist was more snug than I would have liked. The S was a looser trouser look. Also, the coloration from the images isn't exact especially for the brown, it's much more "desaturated." They are stretchy and soft. Almost like fancy sweatpants. I have yet to wash, but due to their composition, I assume the will have the tendency to shrink. The pockets are deep, the quality seems alright. It's an easy to pull fabric, so gentle cycles. No issues with the zipper and button. Opaque, decent length, lightweight. I am 5'6, 128lbs and they sit 2-3 inches above my ankle - I'm pretty used to that. I'll update my review after a few wears.

I’m 5’6, ~150lbs and pear shaped. I originally got the medium but returned it for a large because I wanted the legs to be a little longer. When I washed the large pants the legs did shrink a little bit, but so long as I wear boots or high tops they don’t look like high waters. The pants are extremely comfortable. My state is currently in the high 90’s for summer and I haven’t had any problems with sweating, so I’d said the fabric is pretty breathable. I’ve seen pictures of the pants dressed down, but I wear them to work so they’re easy to dress up with a nice shirt or sweater as well. The pockets are deep for women’s pants. You will need to wear seamless underwear or the creases will show through the back, but if you wear seamless they look great from behind. I actually liked them so much I got a second pair in brown 👍🏻

WOW, these are pretty good!! I am really surprised at how good these are! They fit me (almost) perfectly and they look super cute! I have them on straight out of the package in the pics so they are holding some wrinkles still, but overall, excellent pants and I will be ordering in more colors! Some thoughts below. - Fit is true to size with some flexibility. I am 5'7" and 145lbs and holding on to some extra holiday weight! I ordered my true size small and they are slightly tight but its only noticeable at the pleats since they are not laying flat!! Otherwise, these fit perfectly! The back of the waist has elastic which makes these flexible in terms of sizing. I would say order your true size. - The material is not the softest but its not uncomfortable either. I am in Michigan and they don't have a ton of warmth so not sure these will see much wear until it gets back into the 40's or 50s around here. - I LOVE the jogger look for a business outfit. It adds some fun to the outfit and they are flattering! They could easily be worn with sneakers for a more casual but chic look too! - Love that they have pockets and belt loops! Overall, these are shockingly so good. I will be buying in more colors. True to size.

I would have given these five stars except that the type of material on the outside seems to be picking up every fluffy thing they touch...maybe a lighter colour would be better for that but on the black it shows, so if you buy the black, have your lint roller handy! Overall, I just love these pants and am seriously considering purchasing a second pair in a different colour!

ULTRA COZY!! These camo sweatpants are super comfortable!! The inside is lined with fleece all around, making them perfect for fall and winter! The waistband is about 2” thick which I love!! I ordered the size specified in the manufacturer's images. This was the same size Amazon recommended based on my purchasing history. They fit very comfortably. They are not at all as "barrel shaped" as would seem from the image with the model. The crotch is lower than jeans would be but not awkward, not ill-fitting. I am 5' 7", about 125-130 lbs., and this is the size M. They are a bit shorter than I prefer my pants to be, but I think they will be fine with booties, and I think they look great rolled up. I have no "caboose" and the pants are comfortable on me; if you are *very* curvy in your derriere, these might not suit you, but they'd be worth a try. They are so comfortable and soft. If I was told they were cotton, I would believe it. They're not see-through or flimsy-feeling. I like that the elastic back waist can be hidden with a belt. For pleated corduroys (with which one runs the risk of looking larger than one is) I think the fit is actually flattering. I think the price is slightly high for fabric that is not composed of natural materials (hence four stars instead of five), and the model photo is misleading, but I'm very, very pleased with these and they will get a lot of use this fall. They are great going-out-for-coffee or to-the-pub trousers. Looking forward to pairing them with a vintage, close-fitting sweater, a tweed blazer, and some Born booties. I hope this review was to you. :)

Cute and comfy These pants are cute. I can see them being a regular in my rotation. Do I wish they were a little longer? Yes! I'm 6 ft tall, 136 lbs, and this is a size Large, and longer is always better. But I'd still roll them up and wear them the same way. The fabric is soft and has a deep corduroy pile -- not the super-thick, noisy pile that makes a sound as your legs brush against each other. Just deep enough to feel like true corduroy. The color is a deep, rich dark olive. Very nice.

If you want the fit they're showing, size UP I'm not unhappy with the fit of the pants I got, but it's not anything close to the casual high-waisted wide-legged look in the product photos. For reference, I'm 5'3" with a 29" waist and 40" hips, and I got these in Medium per the size chart. They're the PERFECT length, which is always a pleasant surprise for us shorter humans. And they fit, just not how I expected them to. So if you want that really oversized fit they're showing in all the photos, you will have to size up. They're really comfortable, especially with the elasticized inset at the back of the waistband. The fabric has a bit of give, I wouldn't go so far as to call it stretch but it's not as constricting as a typical corduroy. It's a lighter weight fabric, better suited for cool weather than cold, and if you get one of the lighter colors you will want to be aware of what color undies you put on that day and try to stick to something as close to your skin tone as possible.

Casual Elegance, Light & Comfy These Lace Linen Pants are a big hit with my wife. She loves how they look and feel. They're light and comfy and perfect for Florida weather. They have a lovely design at the calf that really elevates the garment from average to casual elegance. They are a little wrinkly, but that's to be expected of linen and it really doesn't take away from the look. My wife is 5'5 and wears size 10-12. The drawstring waist helps make it adjustable if need be. The large fits perfectly especially if you have a slight belly. They're easy to wash and hang dry and go with almost everything. ~ Kort
